text:
The mix shift under that bar is the real story. Two years ago 95% of AEHR was silicon carbide for EVs. Last quarter AI processors and silicon photonics were over 80% of revenue, up from 56%. Same company, different end market.

Worth noting one of their three 10%+ customers is in optical transceivers. Burn-in is upstream of the transceiver, which is upstream of the switch. When you see AOI breaking ground on 400,000 square feet the same week this backlog prints, you’re looking at one demand signal showing up at two points on the same line.
@Kaizen_Investor Almost bought 200 shares today but hesitated because photonics and anything related has been getting hammered so hard. Go figure

text:
"Is Optical Scale-Up Finally Approaching? NVLink Fusion hints that optics may be moving inside the rack" by Knvul Sheikh. Sheikh is a science journalist who covers on AI, biotechnology and the semiconductor industry.

"Ayar Labs, one of the photonics companies participating in the NVLink Fusion ecosystem, has developed optical chiplets meant to sit alongside GPUs and other processors, converting electrical signals into light only millimeters from the compute silicon. “The most optimal way to do that is having a photonic chiplet with an electronic chiplet and having them hybrid bonded together,” says Ayar’s director of product management, Vishal Chandrasekar.

He argues that optical scale-up has become practical because the semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem has matured around co-packaged optics. Advances in hybrid bonding now allow electronic and photonic chiplets to be manufactured separately, then integrated into a single optical engine that sits beside GPUs or switches.

“What has really happened in the last few years is that the process maturity coming out of the fabs has really improved,” Chandrasekar says. He believes the industry is now on a path toward high-volume optical scale-up systems within the next couple of years.

“Absolutely,” Chandrasekar says. “There are going to be multiple implementations in the 2028 time frame in very high volume.”

text:
Our strength as a nation stems from building the best chips for AI servers, radar, photonic interconnect, etc

That strength is maintained with world-class automation

Cognichip and RFA Electric are both racing to put AI inside chip design itself:

RFA on the analog/RF/photonic side that hasn’t benefitted from automation

Cognichip pushing physics-informed AI across digital and analog/mixed-signal

Privileged to have presenter them to a small group of friends of Lux against a gorgeous backdrop. 🏔️

text:
$AEHR specializes in wafer-level burn-in, a process that stress-tests entire silicon wafers under extreme electrical and thermal conditions before they are cut into individual chips.

$AEHR flagship FOX-XP platform can deliver up to 3,500 watts of electrical power per 300 mm wafer during burn-in.

This is an extraordinary engineering challenge because the system must simultaneously supply massive electrical loads while precisely controlling wafer temperature.

$AEHR proprietary ThinChuck thermal control technology enables this balance, preventing thermal runaway or damage to the silicon while accurately identifying early-life failures.

The platform is also highly scalable.

A single FOX-XP system can be configured with up to 18 test blades, allowing manufacturers to stress-test 18 full wafers in parallel.

This combination of high power density and massive parallelism significantly increases throughput while reducing testing costs for high-volume semiconductor production.

What makes $AEHR particularly attractive as a business is that it doesn't just sell expensive testing systems, it also generates recurring, high-margin revenue from proprietary consumables.

Every customer requires a custom-designed WaferPak Contactor, which creates the electrical connection between the FOX system and each specific wafer design.

Because every chip architecture requires a different contact layout, each new device typically needs its own customized WaferPak.

These contactors experience mechanical wear from repeated compression and degradation from thousands of thermal cycles, meaning they must be replaced regularly.

As customers ramp production of silicon photonics, silicon carbide (SiC), gallium nitride (GaN), AI processors, or other advanced semiconductors, demand for replacement WaferPaks grows alongside wafer volumes.

This creates a high-margin consumables model, the FOX systems drive initial equipment sales, while WaferPak consumables provide recurring, high-margin revenue that supports long-term margin expansion.

$AEHR combines several characteristics that are rare in semiconductor equipment:

Proprietary wafer-level burn-in technology with high barriers to entry.

Ability to test entire wafers at extreme power levels before packaging, reducing downstream manufacturing costs.

High-throughput systems capable of testing up to 18 wafers simultaneously.

Recurring revenue from proprietary WaferPak consumables rather than relying solely on one-time equipment sales.

Exposure to fast-growing markets such as silicon carbide power devices, AI infrastructure, silicon photonics, and advanced semiconductor manufacturing.

The result is a business model that benefits both from semiconductor capital spending and from increasing customer production volume, giving $AEHR leverage to long-term industry growth.

$AEHR flagship FOX-XP platform can deliver up to 3,500 watts of electrical power per 300 mm wafer during burn-in.

This is an extraordinary engineering challenge because the system must simultaneously supply massive electrical loads while precisely controlling wafer temperature.

$AEHR proprietary ThinChuck thermal control technology enables this balance, preventing thermal runaway or damage to the silicon while accurately identifying early-life failures.

The platform is also highly scalable.

A single FOX-XP system can be configured with up to 18 test blades, allowing manufacturers to stress-test 18 full wafers in parallel.

This combination of high power density and massive parallelism significantly increases throughput while reducing testing costs for high-volume semiconductor production.

What makes $AEHR particularly attractive as a business is that it doesn't just sell expensive testing systems, it also generates recurring, high-margin revenue from proprietary consumables.

Every customer requires a custom-designed WaferPak Contactor, which creates the electrical connection between the FOX system and each specific wafer design.

Because every chip architecture requires a different contact layout, each new device typically needs its own customized WaferPak.

These contactors experience mechanical wear from repeated compression and degradation from thousands of thermal cycles, meaning they must be replaced regularly.

As customers ramp production of silicon photonics, silicon carbide (SiC), gallium nitride (GaN), AI processors, or other advanced semiconductors, demand for replacement WaferPaks grows alongside wafer volumes.

This creates a high-margin consumables model, the FOX systems drive initial equipment sales, while WaferPak consumables provide recurring, high-margin revenue that supports long-term margin expansion.

$AEHR combines several characteristics that are rare in semiconductor equipment:

Proprietary wafer-level burn-in technology with high barriers to entry.

Ability to test entire wafers at extreme power levels before packaging, reducing downstream manufacturing costs.

High-throughput systems capable of testing up to 18 wafers simultaneously.

Recurring revenue from proprietary WaferPak consumables rather than relying solely on one-time equipment sales.

It's certainly been a bumpy ride for photonics, but I think this ER is extremely bullish for the sector.  Congrats to the 3000+ investors who own this stock with me on @joinautopilot via my Photonics Is Next portfolio.

When @KawzInvests and I published our photonics testing deep dive, $AEHR had just printed a $10.3M revenue quarter and the market called it a miss. We said to watch the bookings, not the revenue.

Q4 settled it:

- Revenue of $18.8M, up 33% YoY
- Record bookings of $60.7M in a single quarter
- Effective backlog doubled in 90 days
- Back to GAAP profitability

Then the bomb. FY27 revenue guidance of $130M to $150M. That's 160% to 200% growth against $50M this year.

The drivers are exactly what we wrote about:

- Their lead AI customer is shifting all burn-in from system level to wafer level for its accelerators

- A benchmark with a major supplier of AI accelerators and CPUs exceeded expectations. They're now moving toward pilot production and handed $AEHR a second device to evaluate

- The lead silicon photonics customer is ramping with follow-on orders, and their newest customer, a global networking leader, is forecasting additional systems this year.

Remember the structural setup. Every silicon photonics wafer needs burn-in before packaging. $AEHR is still the only company doing this at production scale.

Everyone staring at 60x trailing sales missed that the guide compresses it to ~21x forward.

Super excited for photonics earnings season!"}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2077137269396296107"}}, "rest_id": "2077144125867184508", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "39120", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2077165595045621972", "source": "<a href="https://mobile.twitter.com" rel="nofollow">Twitter Web App</a>", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "3276", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Paperhand shorts means that the short covering could be aggressive if/when it happens

Regardless, don't think it's smart relying on short covering but the photonics sector without any shorts covering also looks very attractive here after large drawdowns across the board 

There are a lot of companies in this theme/sector to research, but for the sake of simplicity it might be best to just buy a photonics ETF since we can't predict which individual names perform well or not

Or, for people who prefer individual stocks, a simple pick for quality would be $LITE

Or a more risky, volatile and perhaps more upside if it plays out: $AAOI

If you are worried about CPO delays, a good way to benefit from photonics and copper is also to be long $CRDO

They point out that short sellers have no real conviction in the short thesis and actually intend to buy the stocks back after covering before these companies smash earnings. Optical stocks have traded down for 1-2 months on two stories: fears that Chinese indium phosphide capacity crashes laser pricing for $LITE $COHR $AAOI, and reports that scale up CPO slips a year. Rosenblatt figures Chinese players add 20-40% to their 2030 InP forecast, but they already model 2030 capacity running 40% behind demand. So even with new supply the industry is still short lasers. They also argue the CPO "delay" is overblown. The process isn't delayed, only the ramp to volume. 2028 is now the inflection year and NPO fills the 2027 gap with nearly identical laser needs. Same photons, same suppliers, same thesis. You might see photonics red right now but the smart money is setting up to reload here ahead of earnings. $AAOI has been hit particularly hard and it's well known Rosenblatt has the highest street PT. So it's nice to see a bullish note here ahead of ER. "We expect AAOI to beat and raise and for 2Q26 to mark a watershed quarter where this starts happening consistently. We believe 2Q26 revenues can beat, and be above $200mn, with 800G sales to Amazon and Oracle as the primary driver of upside. The 3Q26 guidance should be ~$300mn with better GMs versus the ~30% level in 1H26" Can't wait to see photonics explode back soon. $LITE $COHR $AAOI $CIEN $VIAV $AXTI"}}}, "rest_id": "2075617730242470376", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "42262", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2075678604827189268", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "9987", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

drop 🔖 I've never come across anyone on FinX talking about this gem: $NOVT. So… I guess I'll be the one to talk about it. It's one of my favorite companies to own for the long term right now, as they provide a foundational hardware layer for the physical AI / advanced robotics revolution that's about to unfold. ⌁ Momentum $NOVT has crossed into sustained demand acceleration powered by technologies that enable physical intelligence. Every single business unit delivered double-digit bookings growth this quarter. Bookings overall jumped 25% YoY, and the company posted its first positive book-to-bill across the board since 2022; it came in at 1.11. That's real evidence that design wins are converting. ⌁ Physical AI Management highlighted their work in intelligent physical AI solutions for care, warehouse automation, humanoids, and precision robotics. Their servo drives, force-torque sensors, and motion subsystems give robots the ability to perceive and react safely in the real world. CFO Buckley: "We feel it is really superior to anything out there from a competitive perspective." They're involved in the ISO standards that will govern this space and are already working with pretty much everybody in humanoids and leading warehouse players. Buckley: "And you can see we are working with pretty much everybody out there when it comes to the humanoid markets and the leading players in warehouse automation. So we feel really good about that technology. [...] But we could not be better positioned, both industry-wise and customer-wise in hoping to grapple on to that opportunity." The management is clearly telling you that the company is a major physical AI pick and shovel play. The long term potential is MASSIVE. New product sales grew over 80% in the quarter. The vitality index (new product revenues as a percent of total revenues) hit 24% of revenue. These are the metrics that matter when content multiplies across joints and end-effectors as humanoids scale. ⌁ GPU drilling moat One line stood out to me. The CEO (Glastra) explicitly said that their air bearing spindles are the only qualified supplier for drilling the complex boards that power next-generation GPUs. This business is growing at strong double-digit rates with a multi-year trajectory ahead. Most people focus on the silicon. Few think about the mechanical precision layer that actually enables the build-out. $NOVT is directly plugged into the AI infrastructure capex cycle. Wall Street has completely failed to model this vector imo. ⌁ Medical precision engine On the medical side, advanced surgery grew mid-teens last year and should deliver double digits again in 2026. The next-gen insufflators are setting safety and workflow standards, and they're expanding even more into robotic surgery and arthroscopy. Consumables have reached 15% of company revenue with strong growth. text:
AI's Next Bottleneck: Networking & Optics Could Become the Biggest Winners of the AI Infrastructure Cycle
The AI investment narrative has largely centered on GPUs and compute. However, the next phase of the AI supercycle may be determined by an entirely different segment: networking and optical infrastructure.
As AI clusters expand from thousands to millions of accelerators, moving data efficiently becomes just as important as generating compute power. Faster GPUs create little value if they spend time waiting for information to arrive.
That is why the Networking & Optics Landscape deserves close attention.
Current industry data highlights an unprecedented supply-demand imbalance:
Coherent's Indium Phosphide (InP) lasers are reportedly sold out through 2027, supported by an exceptional 4x book-to-bill ratio.
Ciena reached a record $7B order backlog, reflecting sustained demand from hyperscalers and telecom infrastructure.
Lumentum expects approximately 77% revenue growth for FY2026, one of the strongest growth outlooks across semiconductor-adjacent industries.
Unlike many AI segments where additional manufacturing capacity is gradually coming online, optical networking remains a genuine bottleneck. Every new AI data center requires high-speed optical interconnects, silicon photonics, switches, and advanced networking hardware before GPUs can operate at scale.
The companies highlighted in this landscape represent nearly every critical layer of the ecosystem:
Switching & Systems: Cisco, Arista, Ciena
Interconnect Silicon: Marvell, Astera Labs, Credo, Macom
Lasers & Optical Components: Coherent, Lumentum, Corning, Applied Optoelectronics
Manufacturing: Fabrinet
China Optics: Innolight, Eoptolink
Silicon Photonics (Private): Lightmatter, Ayar Labs
Crypto Investors Should Pay Attention
Blockchain investors often focus on tokens directly tied to AI narratives, but the real capital cycle frequently begins with physical infrastructure.
As hyperscalers continue deploying massive AI clusters, companies supplying networking hardware and optical components may capture significant value before downstream AI applications fully mature.
The lesson for crypto markets is familiar: infrastructure tends to outperform during the early stages of transformational technology cycles. The same pattern previously benefited semiconductor manufacturers, cloud providers, and blockchain infrastructure projects.
The AI race is no longer just about faster chips—it's increasingly about moving data at unprecedented speed. Until supply catches demand, networking and optics remain one of the strongest structural themes across the entire AI ecosystem.
#AI #Crypto #Stocks #Tech

However, the next phase of the AI supercycle may be determined by an entirely different segment: networking and optical infrastructure. As AI clusters expand from thousands to millions of accelerators, moving data efficiently becomes just as important as generating compute power. Faster GPUs create little value if they spend time waiting for information to arrive. That is why the Networking & Optics Landscape deserves close attention. Current industry data highlights an unprecedented supply-demand imbalance: Coherent's Indium Phosphide (InP) lasers are reportedly sold out through 2027, supported by an exceptional 4x book-to-bill ratio. Ciena reached a record $7B order backlog, reflecting sustained demand from hyperscalers and telecom infrastructure. Lumentum expects approximately 77% revenue growth for FY2026, one of the strongest growth outlooks across semiconductor-adjacent industries. Unlike many AI segments where additional manufacturing capacity is gradually coming online, optical networking remains a genuine bottleneck. Every new AI data center requires high-speed optical interconnects, silicon photonics, switches, and advanced networking hardware before GPUs can operate at scale. text:
Stock to add to watchlist:

Bookmark this.
 
Robotics

$SYM builds AI-powered robotic systems that automate warehouse pallet handling and inventory for major retailers (+~120% YoY).

$TSLA pioneers electric vehicles, energy solutions, and humanoid robotics through its Optimus platform and full self-driving technology (+~35% YoY).

$OUST develops high-resolution digital lidar sensors for autonomous vehicles, robotics, and industrial applications (+~85% YoY).

$VPG manufactures precision sensors and measurement systems used in robotics, aerospace, and industrial automation (+~45% YoY).

Photonics

$LITE supplies optical components and lasers for high-speed data transmission in AI networks and cloud infrastructure (+~60% YoY).

$AAOI designs and manufactures fiber-optic networking products for data centers and telecommunications (+~340% YoY).

$AXTI produces compound semiconductor substrates used in wireless communications, LEDs, and solar applications (+~400%+ YoY).

$AEHR provides test and burn-in equipment for silicon carbide and other power semiconductors critical to EVs and AI (+~340% YoY).

Space

$ASTS develops a space-based cellular broadband network for direct-to-cell connectivity with standard mobile phones (+~280% YoY).

$RKLB offers dedicated small satellite launches and spacecraft manufacturing with its Electron rocket and Photon platform (+~110% YoY).

$LUNR builds lunar landers and delivers NASA payloads while advancing commercial lunar infrastructure (+~95% YoY).

$PL operates the world’s largest fleet of Earth-imaging satellites for daily global monitoring and analytics (+~160% YoY).

AI Infrastructure

$VRT provides critical power, cooling, and digital infrastructure systems for hyperscale data centers (+~75% YoY).

$NBIS supports utility and power infrastructure essential for large-scale AI computing facilities (limited data, theme up strongly).

$IREN operates renewable-powered data centers and Bitcoin mining while expanding high-performance computing capacity (+~150% YoY).

AI Energy

$OKLO develops advanced small modular nuclear reactors tailored for rapid deployment to power AI data centers (newly public, volatile +high growth).

$VST generates competitive power from nuclear and gas assets to meet surging electricity demand from AI (+~120% YoY).

$CEG operates America’s largest nuclear fleet, positioning it as a key supplier of reliable clean energy for hyperscalers (+~80% YoY).

$SMR commercializes small modular reactors as a scalable, carbon-free solution for energy-intensive AI workloads (strong theme performer).

Power Semis

$MPWR designs high-efficiency analog power management semiconductors for AI servers, EVs, and industrial markets (+~65% YoY).

$NVTS develops gallium nitride power ICs that improve efficiency in AI infrastructure and fast-charging applications (+~255% YoY).

$VICR creates high-performance power modules and systems for data centers, aerospace, and renewable energy (+~50% YoY).

$ON manufactures silicon carbide and intelligent power solutions for electric vehicles and industrial automation (+~40% YoY).

AI Inference

$ARM licenses energy-efficient processor architectures that power most smartphones and emerging AI devices (+~300% YoY).

$AMD delivers high-performance CPUs, GPUs, and accelerators optimized for data center AI inference workloads (+~55% YoY).

$RMBS develops high-speed memory interfaces and chip connectivity solutions for AI servers and computing (+~70% YoY).

$INTC manufactures advanced processors and invests in foundry services to compete in AI hardware (+~25% YoY, recovering).

Quantum

$IONQ builds trapped-ion quantum computers and provides cloud access for quantum algorithm development (+~140% YoY).

$RGTI develops superconducting quantum processors and hybrid quantum-classical computing systems (+~180% YoY).

$INFQ advances cold-atom quantum technologies and quantum sensors for computing and sensing applications (early-stage high volatility).

Rare Earths

$MP operates the Mountain Pass rare earth mine, a major US source of materials for magnets and high-tech applications (+~45% YoY).

$UUUU produces uranium and rare earth elements, strengthening domestic supply chains for energy and defense (+~65% YoY).

$USAR focuses on domestic rare earth mining and processing to reduce reliance on foreign critical minerals (+~130% YoY).

text:
This is the supply chain that is running the entire AI revolution and here is how you can benefit from it (Save this). 

When NVIDIA builds a GPU, the chip itself is only part of the story and those GPUs need to talk to each other at extraordinary speeds, across the same rack, across racks, across entire data centers. 

The question of how data moves between chips is the defining bottleneck of the AI era and the answer is silicon photonics, moving data using light instead of copper wire.

Think of copper as a country road and silicon photonics as a superhighway. 

As AI clusters scale from one rack to hundreds of racks running millions of calculations simultaneously, copper runs out of bandwidth while light does not and here is you can benefit from this.

Coherent ($COHR ) is the dominant supplier of optical transceivers and lasers inside every high-speed AI rack.  

Their Q3 FY2026 revenue hit $1.81 billion, up 21% year over year with datacenter and communications now making up 72% of total revenue.  

They are doubling their indium phosphide wafer capacity by end of year, a manufacturing advantage that produces four times the chips at less than half the cost of older technology.

Marvell ($MRVL) spans both the copper switching layer and the silicon photonics layer through its Celestial AI acquisition, making it the most complete play across the entire networking stack.

Corning ($GLW) is the fiber and every photon traveling between racks and between data centers travels through Corning's glass and they are expanding US fiber production by more than 50% and building three new manufacturing plants.

Fabrinet ($FN) is the contract manufacturer that physically builds the optical modules for Coherent, Lumentum, and others.  

Every time an optical company wins a new NVIDIA contract, Fabrinet's production volume goes up and it is a pure leverage on the entire sector without having to pick a winner between the component makers.

IntelliEPI appears in both the SOI wafer layer and the light source layer of this supply chain, making them a double exposure name across two critical inputs.  

When photonics capacity doubles, EPI wafer demand doubles first and almost no one tracks wafer substrate suppliers at this level.

The thesis is simple as AI demand requires exponentially more data movement between chips, copper cannot keep up and light is the only solution. 

Make sure to follow me @MelvinInvests for more overlooked opportunities in AI infrastructure.

When NVIDIA builds a GPU, the chip itself is only part of the story and those GPUs need to talk to each other at extraordinary speeds, across the same rack, across racks, across entire data centers. The question of how data moves between chips is the defining bottleneck of the AI era and the answer is silicon photonics, moving data using light instead of copper wire. Think of copper as a country road and silicon photonics as a superhighway. As AI clusters scale from one rack to hundreds of racks running millions of calculations simultaneously, copper runs out of bandwidth while light does not and here is you can benefit from this. Coherent ($COHR ) is the dominant supplier of optical transceivers and lasers inside every high-speed AI rack. Their Q3 FY2026 revenue hit $1.81 billion, up 21% year over year with datacenter and communications now making up 72% of total revenue. They are doubling their indium phosphide wafer capacity by end of year, a manufacturing advantage that produces four times the chips at less than half the cost of older technology. Marvell ($MRVL) spans both the copper switching layer and the silicon photonics layer through its Celestial AI acquisition, making it the most complete play across the entire networking stack. Corning ($GLW) is the fiber and every photon traveling between racks and between data centers travels through Corning's glass and they are expanding US fiber production by more than 50% and building three new manufacturing plants. Fabrinet ($FN) is the contract manufacturer that physically builds the optical modules for Coherent, Lumentum, and others. Every time an optical company wins a new NVIDIA contract, Fabrinet's production volume goes up and it is a pure leverage on the entire sector without having to pick a winner between the component makers. IntelliEPI appears in both the SOI wafer layer and the light source layer of this supply chain, making them a double exposure name across two critical inputs. When photonics capacity doubles, EPI wafer demand doubles first and almost no one tracks wafer substrate suppliers at this level. The thesis is simple as AI demand requires exponentially more data movement between chips, copper cannot keep up and light is the only solution. text:
🚨Pendant la ruée vers l’or quantique, qui vend les pioches ?

Si le calcul quantique explose, ce ne sont pas forcément les constructeurs de processeurs qui captureront toute la valeur. 

Comme au XIXe siècle, les vrais gagnants pourraient être ceux qui fournissent les outils indispensables à tous.

Les fournisseurs de l’ombre :

•  Cryogénie : Oxford Instruments, Bluefors et Leiden Cryogenics dominent les réfrigérateurs à dilution qui refroidissent les systèmes supraconducteurs à quelques millikelvins. 

Un goulot d’étranglement critique que personne ne peut contourner facilement.

•  Lasers et photonique : TOPTICA       Photonics, M Squared Lasers, Coherent ($COHR ), Lumentum ($LITE ) et IPG Photonics fournissent les systèmes de précision nécessaires aux ions piégés et atomes neutres.

•  Électronique de contrôle : FormFactor ($FORM ) et ses stations de test cryogéniques, ou MKS Instruments ($MKSI ), sont indispensables pour caractériser et faire fonctionner les puces quantiques.

Ces entreprises vendent à toutes les modalités (supraconducteur, ion piégé, photonique, atome neutre). 

Peu importe qui gagne la course aux qubits, elles restent essentielles.

Dans la révolution quantique, les pioches et les pelles pourraient bien rapporter plus sûrement que l’or lui-même.

Kantique — Le quantique décrypté.

text:
KULICKE & SOFFA INDUSTRIES, INC. ( $KLIC)

Kapsamlı Şirket Değerleme ve Stratejik Analiz 

1. Yönetici Özeti ve Stratejik Yol Ayrımı

Yarı iletken endüstrisinde Moore Kanunu'nun fiziksel sınırlara dayanması ve ön uç (front-end) litografi maliyetlerinin katlanarak artması, performans artışının yeni odağını İleri Seviye Paketleme  ve Heterojen Entegrrasyon süreçlerine kaydırmıştır. Kulicke & Soffa, tarihsel olarak otomotiv, endüstriyel ve tüketici elektroniğine hitap eden olgun tel bağlama pazarında %80’e yakın pazar payıyla ezici bir cash cow yönetmektedir. Ancak şirket, son iki yıldır akademik ve kurumsal analizlere konu olan (örneğin 2025 sonundaki Harvard Business School stratejik vaka analizindeki gibi) çok kritik bir yol ayrımındadır: Olgun pazarlardaki nakit akışını koruyarak, yapay zeka (AI) ve yüksek performanslı bilgi işlem (HPC) çiplerinin mimarisini belirleyen ileri seviye montaj teknolojilerine liderlik etmek.

FY2026 ikinci çeyrek sonuçları ve güncel pazar dinamikleri, KLIC’in bu teknolojik dönüşümü başarıyla gerçekleştirdiğini ve döngüsel bir dipten çıkış (cyclical inflection) dönemine girdiğini doğrulamaktadır. Şirket, karlılığı düşük olan Electronics Assembly iş kolunu tamamen kapatarak odağını netleştirmiş, yapay zeka veri merkezlerindeki bant genişliği kısıtlamalarını çözen Thermo-Compression Bonding ve Silicon Photonics çözümlerinde vites artırmıştır.

2. Finansal Performans ve Döngüsel Kaldıraç Analizi

KLIC’in finansal yapısı, arka uç (back-end) sermaye ekipmanı üreticilerinde görülen yüksek operasyonel kaldıraç karakteristiğini taşımaktadır. Sektörel envanter düzeltmelerinin sona ermesiyle birlikte, gelir artışı marjlar üzerinde çarpan etkisi yaratmaktadır.

Çeyreklik Finansal Gerçekleşmeler ve Dinamikler

-- Gelir İvmesi: Q2 FY2026 net geliri, bir önceki çeyreğe göre %21.5, geçen yılın aynı dönemine göre ise %49.8 artarak 242.6 milyon USD seviyesine ulaşmıştır. Bu rakam, konsensüs beklentisi olan 191.4 milyon USD’yi büyük bir sürprizle geride bırakmıştır.

-- Brüt Kar Marjı Genişlemesi: Brüt marj %49.3 olarak gerçekleşmiştir. Geçen yılın aynı çeyreğindeki %24.9'luk seviyeye kıyasla 2,440 baz puanlık operasyonel genişleme, yüksek marjlı Advanced Solutions ve bellek ürün miksinin devreye girmesiyle açıklanmaktadır.

-- Karlılığa Dönüş: GAAP operasyonel karı 38.5 milyon USD'ye yükselmiştir. Geçen yılın aynı döneminde kaydedilen ağır zararların ardından, Non-GAAP operasyonel kar marjı %19.1’e (46.3 milyon USD) fırlamıştır. Non-GAAP EPS ise 0.79 USD ile beklentilerin 0.12 USD üzerinde gelmiştir.

Q3 ve Q4 FY2026 Öngörüleri

Şirketin resmi Q3 FY2026 kılavuzu, ardışık olarak %28 büyüme ile 310 milyon USD gelir hedeflemektedir. Operasyonel giderlerin (OpEx) 85 milyon USD seviyesine çıkması, üretim hacmini (production ramp) desteklemek için yapılan mühendislik istihdamları ve değişken performans primlerinden kaynaklanmaktadır. Yönetim, Q4 FY2026 döneminde de Q3'e kıyasla %5 ile %10 arasında organik bir büyüme trendinin korunacağını öngörmektedir. Bu doğrultuda FY2026 toplam geliri yaklaşık 1.07 milyar USD bandına doğru ilerlemektedir.

3. Segment Bazında Operasyonel ve Teknolojik Derinleşme

KLIC’in ürün portföyü, yarı iletken dünyasının hem olgun hem de en uç teknolojilerine eş zamanlı hizmet verecek şekilde yeniden yapılandırılmıştır.

-- Olgun Pazarlar: Ball/Wedge Bonding ──> Nakit Üretimi & Hacim (Akıllı Telefon, Otomotiv, Endüstriyel)

-- Seküler Pazarlar: Advanced Solutions ──> TCB, FTC, ProMEM Suite, Vertical Wire (AI, HPC, HBM)

-- İstikrar Segmenti: APS ──> Fabrika Kullanım Oranlarına Bağlı Yedek Parça ve Servis Güvencesi

A. Geleneksel ve Hafif Döngüsel Segmentler

-- Ball Bonding Equipment (Tarihsel Pay: %44.8): Klasik altın ve bakır tel bağlama sistemleridir. Akıllı telefon, PC ve alt segment tüketici elektroniğindeki döngüsel toparlanma, bu segmenti Q2 FY2026'da yeniden ana gelir sürücülerinden biri haline getirmiştir (GenSemi gelirleri çeyreklik %19.4 artışla 148.9 milyon USD olmuştur).

-- Wedge Bonding Equipment (Tarihsel Pay: %16.9): Güç yarı iletkenleri, elektrikli araç (EV) batarya paketleri ve endüstriyel modüllere hizmet eder. Otomotiv sektöründeki elektrifikasyon trendiyle daha stabil bir grafik çizmektedir.

-- Aftermarket Products & Services (APS) (Tarihsel Pay: %23.9): Küresel kurulu makine parkının sarf malzemeleri ve servis hizmetleridir. Donanım satışları düşse dahi, fabrikaların çalışmasına paralel olarak sürekli nakit üreten, döngüselliği en düşük defansif kalkandır.

B. Yeni Nesil Hafıza (Memory) ve Güç Çözümleri (2026 İnovasyonları)

Bellek segmenti, kurumsal SSD (eSSD) ve yüksek performanslı sunucu talebiyle Q2 FY2026'da çeyreklik bazda %93 büyüme kaydederek 31.3 milyon USD'ye ulaşmıştır. Şirket bu ivmeyi kalıcı kılmak adına Mart 2026'da iki büyük teknolojik silahını duyurmuştur:

1. ProMEM Suite: Standart yüksek hacimli bellek montajında (Stacked NAND) üretkenliği ve proses kontrolünü dramatik şekilde artıran bir ball bonding yazılım ve donanım paketidir.

2. Vertical Wire Teknolojisi (ATP MEM PLUS): Geleneksel tel bağlamayı dikey boyuta taşıyarak ara bağlantı yoğunluğunu artırır ve paket ayak izini küçültür. Stacked DRAM ve yeni nesil yüksek yoğunluklu bellek mimarilerinde silikon delikli geçişlere (TSV) karşı son derece maliyet etkin bir alternatif sunmaktadır.

4. Gelişmiş Paketleme ve AI Altyapı Katalizörü

Advanced Solutions segmenti altındaki Thermo-Compression Bonding (TCB), KLIC'in uzun vadeli seküler büyüme motorudur. Şirket, TCB iş kolunun FY2026’da çeyreklik bazda en az %70 büyüyeceğini ve tek başına 100 milyon USD+ barajını aşacağını doğrulamıştır.

Fluxless TCB (FTC) ve Teknik Üstünlük

Gelişmiş veri merkezi işlemcilerinde kullanılan alt bileşenlerin kalınlıkları azaldıkça ve kalıp boyutları büyüdükçe (30 mm ve üzeri), geleneksel "flip-chip" yöntemleri termal eğrilme ve hizalama hatalarına neden olmaktadır. KLIC’in Fluxless TCB (FTC) teknolojisi bu problemi şu inovasyonlarla çözmektedir:

-- Mikron Altı Hassasiyet: Lehim tabanlı bağlantıları 10 μm hatveye (pitch), doğrudan bakır-bakır bağlantıları ise en az 5 μm hatveye kadar indirebilmektedir.

-- Formik Asit Buharı Oksit İndirgeme: Süreç esnasında sıvı akı (flux) kullanılmadığı için kalıplar arasında neredeyse "sıfır boşluk yüksekliği" elde edilir; bu da elektriksel direnci düşürür ve termal yönetimi optimize eder.

-- Co-Packaged Optics ve Silikon Fotonik: Yapay zeka kümelerindeki anahtarlama ve ağ hızlarını artırmak için optik transceiver bileşenlerinin doğrudan işlemci yanına TCB ile montajlanması sürecinde KLIC, küresel lider dökümhanelerden ve OSAT'lardan peş peşe siparişler almaktadır.

5. Kapasite Genişletme ve Gelecek Projeksiyonları

Advanced Solutions segmentinin ölçeklenebilmesi için üretim darboğazlarının aşılması gerekmektedir. Şirket yönetimi bu doğrultuda stratejik bir sermaye hamlesi yapmıştır:

1. CapEx Artırımı
CapEx Artırımı:FY2026 Dönemi.Şirket, yıllık sermaye harcaması (CapEx) bütçesini 12 milyon USD seviyesinden 22 milyon USD seviyesine neredeyse iki katına çıkarmıştır.

2. Üretim Hatlarının Modernizasyonu:
Q3/Q4 FY2026. Artırılan bütçe doğrudan Singapur ve ilgili üretim tesislerinde Fluxless TCB ve dikey tel montaj hatlarının otomasyon kalitesini yükseltmek için kullanılmaktadır.

3.Kapasite Hedefine Ulaşılması:
Erken FY2027. Yapılan bu yatırımlarla Advanced Solutions segmentinin yıllık üretim ve sevkiyat kapasitesi run-rate bazında 400 milyon USD satış potansiyeline ulaştırılacaktır.

Sipariş birikimini gösteren backlog yapısı da bu süreci desteklemektedir ve hızla artmakdadır. Siparişlerin iptal edilebilir olması bir risk olsa da Çin, Tayvan ve Kore'deki fabrika kullanım oranlarının %90'ın üzerinde seyretmesi, bu siparişlerin büyük ölçüde realizasyonla sonuçlanacağını göstermektedir.

6. Sektörel Karşılaştırma ve İleri Dönük Değerleme Modeli

Yarı iletken ekipman pazarında değerleme yapılırken ön uç (ASML, KLA, Lam Research, Applied Materials) ve arka uç (KLIC, Besi, ASMPT) ayrımı kritik rol oynar. Ön uç firmaları daha az döngüsel ve yüksek bariyerli oldukları için tarihsel olarak yüksek çarpanlara sahiptir.

KLIC ise tarihsel olarak olgun wire bonder pazarının dalgalanmalarına maruz kaldığı için düşük çarpanla işlem görmüştür. Fakat Advanced Solutions segmentinin kapasitesinin 400 milyon USD'ye ayarlanması ve TCB'nin yüksek karlılığı, çarpan genişlemesini tetiklemektedir.

Akran Grubu Karşılaştırma Analizi (FOTO - 1)

2027 Gelir ve Çarpan Projeksiyonu

-- FY2026 Tahmini Gelir: ~1.07 milyar USD (Mevcut çeyreklik gidişat ve Q3 kılavuzu tabanlı).

-- FY2027 Projeksiyon Geliri: Bellek toparlanması, ProMEM Suite adaptasyonu ve TCB'nin yıllık 100 milyon USD'lik barajı kırıp Advanced Solutions run-rate'ini yukarı taşımasıyla 1.25 milyar USD (%17-20 civarı bir yıllık büyüme).

-- Değerleme Yaklaşımı: Advanced Packaging payının artması sebebiyle şirkete hak ettiği tarihsel iskontonun üzerinde, 6x Forward P/S (İleri Dönük Satış) çarpanı uygulanması rasyoneldir. Bu çarpan altında şirketin adil kurumsal değeri 7.5 milyar USD olarak hesaplanmaktadır.

Şirketin kasasındaki 254.3 milyon USD net nakit ve sıfır borç yapısı, hisse başına değer hesaplamasında ekstra bir emniyet marjı sunmaktadır. Finansal modelleme kapsamında %8 iskonto oranı tatbik edildiğinde, hisse senedinin önümüzdeki 12 aylık periyotta 135 USD adil değer hedefine doğru re-rate edilmesi beklenmektedir. Bu da mevcut konsolide fiyata göre yaklaşık %20 yukarı potansiyel anlamına gelir.

7. Derinlemesine Risk ve Tehdit Analizi

Risk Matrisi ve Stratejik Tehditler: KLIC pozisyonlanmasında aşağı yönlü risklerin yakından izlenmesi gerekmektedir.

I. İkame Teknolojiler ve Hibrit Bağlama Tehdidi

En uç seviye AI hızlandırıcılarında (NVIDIA, AMD vb. yeni nesil mimarileri) TCB hatvelerinin dahi yetersiz kaldığı noktalarda Hybrid Bonding (Doğrudan Bakır-Bakır Die-to-Wafer entegrasyonu) devreye girmektedir. Bu alanda Hollandalı BE Semiconductor (Besi) tartışmasız pazar lideridir ve ana dökümhanelerin (TSMC vb.) onaylı tedarikçisidir.

Risk: Eğer Hybrid Bonding teknolojisi beklenenden daha hızlı ucuzlar ve orta segment çiplere de hızla adapte edilirse, KLIC'in TCB pazarı daralabilir. KLIC, 2024'ten bu yana kendi bünyesindeki Hibrit Bağlama AR-GE programını hızlandırdığını ve erken müşteri test aşamasında olduğunu (Mart 2026 güncellemelerinde) belirtse de Besi ile olan teknolojik ve zamansal makası kapatamama riski mevcuttur.

II. Müşteri Konsantrasyonu ve Kamçı Etkisi
KLIC’in gelir yapısı son derece dikeydir. En büyük 10 müşteri (büyük OSAT'lar olan ASE, Amkor ve dev IDM'ler) toplam grup gelirlerinin %55'ini oluşturmaktadır.

Risk: Son tüketici elektroniğindeki ani bir makroekonomik durgunluk, bu 10 dev firmanın CapEx bütçelerini anında dondurmasına yol açar. Arka uç üreticileri, tedarik zincirinin en sonunda yer aldığından "Kamçı Etkisi" nedeniyle sipariş iptallerini veya ertelemelerini en şiddetli hisseden grup olmaktadır.

III. Jeopolitik Sıkışma ve Doğu Asya Riski

KLIC her ne kadar Singapur merkezli ve ABD operasyonlu bir yapıya sahip olsa da en büyük yarı iletken montaj müşterileri ve dökümhaneleri Tayvan, Çin Halk Cumhuriyeti ve Güney Kore sınırları içerisindedir.

Risk: ABD-Çin ticaret savaşlarının gelişmiş paketleme ekipmanlarına (özellikle TCB ve alt bileşenlerine) yönelik getirebileceği ek ihracat yasakları veya Tayvan Boğazı'ndaki olası bir lojistik aksama, şirketin backlog teslimatlarını tamamen felç edebilir.

IV. CapEx ve Marj Baskısı

Yıllık CapEx'in 12 milyon USD'den 22 milyon USD'ye çıkarılması, kısa vadede nakit akışını ve serbest nakit akışı marjlarını (Q2 FY2026'da düzeltilmiş serbest nakit akışı 6.3 milyon USD seviyesindedir) baskılamaktadır. Yeni hatların verimlilik oranlarında yaşanabilecek herhangi bir operasyonel aksaklık, Q3 ve Q4 brüt marj tahminlerini aşağı yönlü revize ettirebilir.

8. Stratejik Sonuç

Kulicke & Soffa, döngüsel bir çöküşün ardından operasyonel karlılığa dönüş hikayesini başarıyla yazmıştır. Şirket, geleneksel iş kollarının sağladığı güçlü nakit akışını, geleceğin AI ve bellek standartları olan TCB, ProMEM ve dikey tel teknolojilerine başarıyla aktarmaktadır. Hisse senedinde son aylarda yaşanan güçlü momentum ve ralli, bu döngüsel dönüşün pazar tarafından satın alınmaya başladığını gösterse de FY2027 projeksiyonlarımız doğrultusunda şirketin çarpan büyümesinin henüz tamamlanmadığı değerlendirilmektedir. Bilançosunda borç bulunmayan, düzenli çeyreklik temettü ödeyen ve hisse geri alımlarıyla paydaş değerini destekleyen KLIC, uzun vadeli yarı iletken portföylerinde temel bir arka uç pozisyonu olarak öne çıkmaktadır.

Kapsamlı Şirket Değerleme ve Stratejik Analiz 

1. Yönetici Özeti ve Stratejik Yol Ayrımı

Yarı iletken endüstrisinde Moore Kanunu'nun fiziksel sınırlara dayanması ve ön uç (front-end) litografi maliyetlerinin katlanarak artması, performans artışının yeni odağını İleri Seviye Paketleme  ve Heterojen Entegrrasyon süreçlerine kaydırmıştır. Kulicke & Soffa, tarihsel olarak otomotiv, endüstriyel ve tüketici elektroniğine hitap eden olgun tel bağlama pazarında %80’e yakın pazar payıyla ezici bir cash cow yönetmektedir. Ancak şirket, son iki yıldır akademik ve kurumsal analizlere konu olan (örneğin 2025 sonundaki Harvard Business School stratejik vaka analizindeki gibi) çok kritik bir yol ayrımındadır: Olgun pazarlardaki nakit akışını koruyarak, yapay zeka (AI) ve yüksek performanslı bilgi işlem (HPC) çiplerinin mimarisini belirleyen ileri seviye montaj teknolojilerine liderlik etmek.

FY2026 ikinci çeyrek sonuçları ve güncel pazar dinamikleri, KLIC’in bu teknolojik dönüşümü başarıyla gerçekleştirdiğini ve döngüsel bir dipten çıkış (cyclical inflection) dönemine girdiğini doğrulamaktadır. Şirket, karlılığı düşük olan Electronics Assembly iş kolunu tamamen kapatarak odağını netleştirmiş, yapay zeka veri merkezlerindeki bant genişliği kısıtlamalarını çözen Thermo-Compression Bonding ve Silicon Photonics çözümlerinde vites artırmıştır.

2. Finansal Performans ve Döngüsel Kaldıraç Analizi

KLIC’in finansal yapısı, arka uç (back-end) sermaye ekipmanı üreticilerinde görülen yüksek operasyonel kaldıraç karakteristiğini taşımaktadır. Sektörel envanter düzeltmelerinin sona ermesiyle birlikte, gelir artışı marjlar üzerinde çarpan etkisi yaratmaktadır.

Çeyreklik Finansal Gerçekleşmeler ve Dinamikler

-- Gelir İvmesi: Q2 FY2026 net geliri, bir önceki çeyreğe göre %21.5, geçen yılın aynı dönemine göre ise %49.8 artarak 242.6 milyon USD seviyesine ulaşmıştır. Bu rakam, konsensüs beklentisi olan 191.4 milyon USD’yi büyük bir sürprizle geride bırakmıştır.

-- Brüt Kar Marjı Genişlemesi: Brüt marj %49.3 olarak gerçekleşmiştir. Geçen yılın aynı çeyreğindeki %24.9'luk seviyeye kıyasla 2,440 baz puanlık operasyonel genişleme, yüksek marjlı Advanced Solutions ve bellek ürün miksinin devreye girmesiyle açıklanmaktadır.

-- Karlılığa Dönüş: GAAP operasyonel karı 38.5 milyon USD'ye yükselmiştir. Geçen yılın aynı döneminde kaydedilen ağır zararların ardından, Non-GAAP operasyonel kar marjı %19.1’e (46.3 milyon USD) fırlamıştır. Non-GAAP EPS ise 0.79 USD ile beklentilerin 0.12 USD üzerinde gelmiştir.

Q3 ve Q4 FY2026 Öngörüleri

Şirketin resmi Q3 FY2026 kılavuzu, ardışık olarak %28 büyüme ile 310 milyon USD gelir hedeflemektedir. Operasyonel giderlerin (OpEx) 85 milyon USD seviyesine çıkması, üretim hacmini (production ramp) desteklemek için yapılan mühendislik istihdamları ve değişken performans primlerinden kaynaklanmaktadır. Yönetim, Q4 FY2026 döneminde de Q3'e kıyasla %5 ile %10 arasında organik bir büyüme trendinin korunacağını öngörmektedir. Bu doğrultuda FY2026 toplam geliri yaklaşık 1.07 milyar USD bandına doğru ilerlemektedir.

3. Segment Bazında Operasyonel ve Teknolojik Derinleşme

KLIC’in ürün portföyü, yarı iletken dünyasının hem olgun hem de en uç teknolojilerine eş zamanlı hizmet verecek şekilde yeniden yapılandırılmıştır.

-- Olgun Pazarlar: Ball/Wedge Bonding ──> Nakit Üretimi & Hacim (Akıllı Telefon, Otomotiv, Endüstriyel)

-- Seküler Pazarlar: Advanced Solutions ──> TCB, FTC, ProMEM Suite, Vertical Wire (AI, HPC, HBM)

-- İstikrar Segmenti: APS ──> Fabrika Kullanım Oranlarına Bağlı Yedek Parça ve Servis Güvencesi

A. Geleneksel ve Hafif Döngüsel Segmentler

-- Ball Bonding Equipment (Tarihsel Pay: %44.8): Klasik altın ve bakır tel bağlama sistemleridir. Akıllı telefon, PC ve alt segment tüketici elektroniğindeki döngüsel toparlanma, bu segmenti Q2 FY2026'da yeniden ana gelir sürücülerinden biri haline getirmiştir (GenSemi gelirleri çeyreklik %19.4 artışla 148.9 milyon USD olmuştur).

-- Wedge Bonding Equipment (Tarihsel Pay: %16.9): Güç yarı iletkenleri, elektrikli araç (EV) batarya paketleri ve endüstriyel modüllere hizmet eder. Otomotiv sektöründeki elektrifikasyon trendiyle daha stabil bir grafik çizmektedir.

-- Aftermarket Products & Services (APS) (Tarihsel Pay: %23.9): Küresel kurulu makine parkının sarf malzemeleri ve servis hizmetleridir. Donanım satışları düşse dahi, fabrikaların çalışmasına paralel olarak sürekli nakit üreten, döngüselliği en düşük defansif kalkandır.

B. Yeni Nesil Hafıza (Memory) ve Güç Çözümleri (2026 İnovasyonları)

Bellek segmenti, kurumsal SSD (eSSD) ve yüksek performanslı sunucu talebiyle Q2 FY2026'da çeyreklik bazda %93 büyüme kaydederek 31.3 milyon USD'ye ulaşmıştır. Şirket bu ivmeyi kalıcı kılmak adına Mart 2026'da iki büyük teknolojik silahını duyurmuştur:

1. ProMEM Suite: Standart yüksek hacimli bellek montajında (Stacked NAND) üretkenliği ve proses kontrolünü dramatik şekilde artıran bir ball bonding yazılım ve donanım paketidir.

2. Vertical Wire Teknolojisi (ATP MEM PLUS): Geleneksel tel bağlamayı dikey boyuta taşıyarak ara bağlantı yoğunluğunu artırır ve paket ayak izini küçültür. Stacked DRAM ve yeni nesil yüksek yoğunluklu bellek mimarilerinde silikon delikli geçişlere (TSV) karşı son derece maliyet etkin bir alternatif sunmaktadır.

4. Gelişmiş Paketleme ve AI Altyapı Katalizörü

Advanced Solutions segmenti altındaki Thermo-Compression Bonding (TCB), KLIC'in uzun vadeli seküler büyüme motorudur. Şirket, TCB iş kolunun FY2026’da çeyreklik bazda en az %70 büyüyeceğini ve tek başına 100 milyon USD+ barajını aşacağını doğrulamıştır.

Fluxless TCB (FTC) ve Teknik Üstünlük

Gelişmiş veri merkezi işlemcilerinde kullanılan alt bileşenlerin kalınlıkları azaldıkça ve kalıp boyutları büyüdükçe (30 mm ve üzeri), geleneksel "flip-chip" yöntemleri termal eğrilme ve hizalama hatalarına neden olmaktadır. KLIC’in Fluxless TCB (FTC) teknolojisi bu problemi şu inovasyonlarla çözmektedir:

-- Mikron Altı Hassasiyet: Lehim tabanlı bağlantıları 10 μm hatveye (pitch), doğrudan bakır-bakır bağlantıları ise en az 5 μm hatveye kadar indirebilmektedir.

-- Formik Asit Buharı Oksit İndirgeme: Süreç esnasında sıvı akı (flux) kullanılmadığı için kalıplar arasında neredeyse "sıfır boşluk yüksekliği" elde edilir; bu da elektriksel direnci düşürür ve termal yönetimi optimize eder.

-- Co-Packaged Optics ve Silikon Fotonik: Yapay zeka kümelerindeki anahtarlama ve ağ hızlarını artırmak için optik transceiver bileşenlerinin doğrudan işlemci yanına TCB ile montajlanması sürecinde KLIC, küresel lider dökümhanelerden ve OSAT'lardan peş peşe siparişler almaktadır.

5. Kapasite Genişletme ve Gelecek Projeksiyonları

Advanced Solutions segmentinin ölçeklenebilmesi için üretim darboğazlarının aşılması gerekmektedir. Şirket yönetimi bu doğrultuda stratejik bir sermaye hamlesi yapmıştır:

1. CapEx Artırımı
CapEx Artırımı:FY2026 Dönemi.Şirket, yıllık sermaye harcaması (CapEx) bütçesini 12 milyon USD seviyesinden 22 milyon USD seviyesine neredeyse iki katına çıkarmıştır.

2. Üretim Hatlarının Modernizasyonu:
Q3/Q4 FY2026. Artırılan bütçe doğrudan Singapur ve ilgili üretim tesislerinde Fluxless TCB ve dikey tel montaj hatlarının otomasyon kalitesini yükseltmek için kullanılmaktadır.

3.Kapasite Hedefine Ulaşılması:
Erken FY2027. Yapılan bu yatırımlarla Advanced Solutions segmentinin yıllık üretim ve sevkiyat kapasitesi run-rate bazında 400 milyon USD satış potansiyeline ulaştırılacaktır.

Sipariş birikimini gösteren backlog yapısı da bu süreci desteklemektedir ve hızla artmakdadır. Siparişlerin iptal edilebilir olması bir risk olsa da Çin, Tayvan ve Kore'deki fabrika kullanım oranlarının %90'ın üzerinde seyretmesi, bu siparişlerin büyük ölçüde realizasyonla sonuçlanacağını göstermektedir.

6. Sektörel Karşılaştırma ve İleri Dönük Değerleme Modeli

Yarı iletken ekipman pazarında değerleme yapılırken ön uç (ASML, KLA, Lam Research, Applied Materials) ve arka uç (KLIC, Besi, ASMPT) ayrımı kritik rol oynar. Ön uç firmaları daha az döngüsel ve yüksek bariyerli oldukları için tarihsel olarak yüksek çarpanlara sahiptir.

KLIC ise tarihsel olarak olgun wire bonder pazarının dalgalanmalarına maruz kaldığı için düşük çarpanla işlem görmüştür. Fakat Advanced Solutions segmentinin kapasitesinin 400 milyon USD'ye ayarlanması ve TCB'nin yüksek karlılığı, çarpan genişlemesini tetiklemektedir.

Akran Grubu Karşılaştırma Analizi (FOTO - 1)

2027 Gelir ve Çarpan Projeksiyonu

-- FY2026 Tahmini Gelir: ~1.07 milyar USD (Mevcut çeyreklik gidişat ve Q3 kılavuzu tabanlı).

-- FY2027 Projeksiyon Geliri: Bellek toparlanması, ProMEM Suite adaptasyonu ve TCB'nin yıllık 100 milyon USD'lik barajı kırıp Advanced Solutions run-rate'ini yukarı taşımasıyla 1.25 milyar USD (%17-20 civarı bir yıllık büyüme).

-- Değerleme Yaklaşımı: Advanced Packaging payının artması sebebiyle şirkete hak ettiği tarihsel iskontonun üzerinde, 6x Forward P/S (İleri Dönük Satış) çarpanı uygulanması rasyoneldir. Bu çarpan altında şirketin adil kurumsal değeri 7.5 milyar USD olarak hesaplanmaktadır.

Şirketin kasasındaki 254.3 milyon USD net nakit ve sıfır borç yapısı, hisse başına değer hesaplamasında ekstra bir emniyet marjı sunmaktadır. Finansal modelleme kapsamında %8 iskonto oranı tatbik edildiğinde, hisse senedinin önümüzdeki 12 aylık periyotta 135 USD adil değer hedefine doğru re-rate edilmesi beklenmektedir. Bu da mevcut konsolide fiyata göre yaklaşık %20 yukarı potansiyel anlamına gelir.

7. Derinlemesine Risk ve Tehdit Analizi

Risk Matrisi ve Stratejik Tehditler: KLIC pozisyonlanmasında aşağı yönlü risklerin yakından izlenmesi gerekmektedir.

I. İkame Teknolojiler ve Hibrit Bağlama Tehdidi

En uç seviye AI hızlandırıcılarında (NVIDIA, AMD vb. yeni nesil mimarileri) TCB hatvelerinin dahi yetersiz kaldığı noktalarda Hybrid Bonding (Doğrudan Bakır-Bakır Die-to-Wafer entegrasyonu) devreye girmektedir. Bu alanda Hollandalı BE Semiconductor (Besi) tartışmasız pazar lideridir ve ana dökümhanelerin (TSMC vb.) onaylı tedarikçisidir.

Risk: Eğer Hybrid Bonding teknolojisi beklenenden daha hızlı ucuzlar ve orta segment çiplere de hızla adapte edilirse, KLIC'in TCB pazarı daralabilir. KLIC, 2024'ten bu yana kendi bünyesindeki Hibrit Bağlama AR-GE programını hızlandırdığını ve erken müşteri test aşamasında olduğunu (Mart 2026 güncellemelerinde) belirtse de Besi ile olan teknolojik ve zamansal makası kapatamama riski mevcuttur.

II. Müşteri Konsantrasyonu ve Kamçı Etkisi
KLIC’in gelir yapısı son derece dikeydir. En büyük 10 müşteri (büyük OSAT'lar olan ASE, Amkor ve dev IDM'ler) toplam grup gelirlerinin %55'ini oluşturmaktadır.

Risk: Son tüketici elektroniğindeki ani bir makroekonomik durgunluk, bu 10 dev firmanın CapEx bütçelerini anında dondurmasına yol açar. Arka uç üreticileri, tedarik zincirinin en sonunda yer aldığından "Kamçı Etkisi" nedeniyle sipariş iptallerini veya ertelemelerini en şiddetli hisseden grup olmaktadır.

III. Jeopolitik Sıkışma ve Doğu Asya Riski

KLIC her ne kadar Singapur merkezli ve ABD operasyonlu bir yapıya sahip olsa da en büyük yarı iletken montaj müşterileri ve dökümhaneleri Tayvan, Çin Halk Cumhuriyeti ve Güney Kore sınırları içerisindedir.

Risk: ABD-Çin ticaret savaşlarının gelişmiş paketleme ekipmanlarına (özellikle TCB ve alt bileşenlerine) yönelik getirebileceği ek ihracat yasakları veya Tayvan Boğazı'ndaki olası bir lojistik aksama, şirketin backlog teslimatlarını tamamen felç edebilir.

IV. CapEx ve Marj Baskısı

Yıllık CapEx'in 12 milyon USD'den 22 milyon USD'ye çıkarılması, kısa vadede nakit akışını ve serbest nakit akışı marjlarını (Q2 FY2026'da düzeltilmiş serbest nakit akışı 6.3 milyon USD seviyesindedir) baskılamaktadır. Yeni hatların verimlilik oranlarında yaşanabilecek herhangi bir operasyonel aksaklık, Q3 ve Q4 brüt marj tahminlerini aşağı yönlü revize ettirebilir.

8. Stratejik Sonuç

Kulicke & Soffa, döngüsel bir çöküşün ardından operasyonel karlılığa dönüş hikayesini başarıyla yazmıştır. Şirket, geleneksel iş kollarının sağladığı güçlü nakit akışını, geleceğin AI ve bellek standartları olan TCB, ProMEM ve dikey tel teknolojilerine başarıyla aktarmaktadır. Hisse senedinde son aylarda yaşanan güçlü momentum ve ralli, bu döngüsel dönüşün pazar tarafından satın alınmaya başladığını gösterse de FY2027 projeksiyonlarımız doğrultusunda şirketin çarpan büyümesinin henüz tamamlanmadığı değerlendirilmektedir. Bilançosunda borç bulunmayan, düzenli çeyreklik temettü ödeyen ve hisse geri alımlarıyla paydaş değerini destekleyen KLIC, uzun vadeli yarı iletken portföylerinde temel bir arka uç pozisyonu olarak öne çıkmaktadır.

text:
Sometimes I forget how rare investors like us are. 

I know many people in my country aren't even investing and have all their wealth on a savings account.

Then you have people who do invest, but only in indices. They add money occasionally and for the rest they never look back.

Then you have people who invest in stocks, but only the safe ones or dividend stocks, like $KO, $MCD, $OXY, etc.

And then you have us. Investors in AI, memory, photonics, space, you name it, with a very high amount of conviction. I've seen my portfolio go up or down over 10% on a single day several times this year and I barely flinch at this point.

We are a rare species.

text:
$BURU Not financial advice. Not too often a penny stock catches my eye. the pivot from blue lasers to infusing those blue laser capabilities into counter UAS, drones, defense, software, photonics all under one roof to go along with their NATO ties for pennies on the dollar is quite fascinating. 

A lot of risks with the financial health that's true but upside could be worth it at these levels (currently $0.1481 per share ) Loaded 2100 shares yesterday at $0.1417. The more I research the more I'm thinking I should've bought more. Probably 10x my share count these next couple weeks.  

A lot of regret not catching $ONDS earlier (entered at $8). I personally like the defense sector as a whole at these low valuations, but $BURU seems like quite the sleeper.

Management is outlining a more than $300 million development plan to build an integrated defense and advanced technology platform, shifting the company away from being known primarily as a laser technology business. Think of it as a complete business transformation. Instead of relying mainly on industrial laser products, Nuburu wants to combine defense electronics, secure communications, AI, cybersecurity, drones, advanced manufacturing, and military technologies under one organization. If executed successfully, the company could evolve into a national defense technology platform rather than a niche hardware supplier. The timing is also important. Europe is rapidly increasing defense spending as NATO members commit to higher military budgets. Governments are looking to strengthen domestic defense supply chains instead of relying heavily on foreign suppliers. Tekne already has relationships in Italy’s defense sector, giving Nuburu an entry point into this growing market. Think about companies like $PLTR, $KTOS, $LHX, $LMT, $NOC, and $RTX. These businesses have generated strong long-term growth by providing software, autonomous systems, communications, sensors, and defense technologies. Nuburu appears to be positioning itself to participate in a similar ecosystem, although at a much earlier stage. The opportunity is significant because defense contracts are typically multi-year, recurring, and government-backed, providing greater revenue visibility than many commercial markets. However, investors should also recognize the risks. A turnaround of this size requires integrating acquisitions, securing new contracts, raising capital when needed, and successfully executing a completely new strategy. Until the company begins winning meaningful defense contracts and generating sustainable revenue, much of today’s valuation will depend on investor confidence in management’s execution. Bottom line: Investors are becoming interested because Nuburu is no longer being viewed solely as a laser company. If management successfully transforms it into an integrated European defense and advanced technology platform, the company’s long-term opportunity could be substantially larger than its legacy business. The next major catalysts will be contract wins, acquisition integration, government partnerships, and evidence that the $300 million development plan is translating into real revenue growth."}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2074769379749748791"}}, "rest_id": "2074783189860753420", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2357", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2076006116652102041", "source": "Twitter for Android", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1426", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
morgan stanley dropped a CPO note today and the laser supply chain is a lot more concentrated than most people realize

the three western names that actually matter here: $SIVE, COHR, LITE

sitting next to switch platforms from broadcom and nvidia. optical engines from lightmatter, ayar labs, marvell/celestial, poet. silicon photonics foundry capacity at TSM, GFS, TSEM

rosenblatt put out a separate note saying chinese laser suppliers are nowhere close on CPO-grade lasers. that matters

so you've got a $1.5B company in SIVE sitting next to two $60B+ giants as one of the critical global players. morgan stanley basically validated what a few of us have been saying about the laser chokepoint

not many ways to play CPO at the component level. this is one of them

text:
$AEHR deep dive:

Financials are ugly backward-looking, but interesting forward-looking. TTM revenue was USD 45.26M, TTM gross margin was 30.72%, free cash flow was negative USD 12.19M, and FY2027 consensus revenue was USD 85.14M, or about 71% growth.

The thesis is that Aehr is becoming a tollbooth for testing the fragile stuff inside AI hardware: high-power AI ASICs, advanced packages, silicon photonics, and optical I/O. If these chips fail inside a data-center system, the customer has a very expensive bad day.

The catalysts are real: Q3 FY2026 bookings were USD 37.2M, effective backlog was USD 50.9M, then Aehr announced a record USD 41M hyperscale AI package-level burn-in order. On June 17, it added a follow-on FOX-XP wafer-level burn-in system order from a major silicon-photonics customer, configured for nine wafers in parallel and scheduled for delivery within six months.

The catch: the new SiPh order value and customer name were not disclosed, and the customer forecast for more systems this year is not backlog until purchase orders show up. At roughly USD 112, the stock was already around USD 3B-plus market cap, with stale analyst targets around USD 64. That is not exactly a bargain bin.

Risks: valuation, customer concentration, bookings-to-revenue timing, gross margin recovery, and the classic small-cap semi equipment problem where everyone falls in love with the order headline before the revenue and margin proof arrives.

Entry/Verdict: HOLD / catalyst watch / no add-size chase. Tiny tracker only if you want event exposure. Real add-size waits for FY2027 guidance above USD 100M-125M minimum, ideally toward USD 140M+, plus backlog conversion, margin recovery, and estimate revisions.

The tech signal is real. The stock just needs the numbers to stop being theoretical. We'll see how it goes.

text:
Morgan Stanley’s CPO report supports one of the more important AI infrastructure ideas:

The next bottleneck may not be compute.

It may be optical connectivity.

Key names across the CPO stack:

$SIVE $COHR $LITE — lasers
$AVGO $NVDA — switch platforms
Lightmatter / Ayar Labs / $MRVL / POET — photonics
$TSM $GFS $TSEM — silicon photonics foundry capacity
The company I keep coming back to is $SIVE.
A ~$1.5B laser supplier positioned inside a strategic bottleneck where Western leadership may matter more as CPO adoption scales.

text:
The CPO supply chain is starting to look like a serious AI infrastructure theme.

Morgan Stanley’s latest report highlights:

• Laser supply chain: $SIVE $COHR $LITE
• Switch platforms: $AVGO $NVDA
• Optical engines: Lightmatter, Ayar Labs, $MRVL, Celestial, POET
• Silicon photonics foundries: $TSM $GFS $TSEM
My focus remains on $SIVE.
Why?
Because CPO needs high-performance laser suppliers, and Rosenblatt’s view suggests Chinese competitors are still far from meeting those requirements.

That makes Western laser companies much more important than the market may realize.

text:
AI infrastructure is not just GPUs.

CPO could become one of the most important bottlenecks in next-generation AI networking.

Morgan Stanley’s latest CPO report points to several key players:

$SIVE $COHR $LITE — laser supply chain
$AVGO $NVDA — switch platforms
Lightmatter / Ayar Labs / $MRVL / POET — optical engines
$TSM $GFS $TSEM — silicon photonics capacity
The standout is $SIVE.
At around $1.5B, it is small compared with the scale of the AI infrastructure buildout, but its laser role may become increasingly critical.

text:
Morgan Stanley’s latest CPO report lines up closely with the research I’ve been tracking.

Key CPO beneficiaries appear across the stack:

🔹 Lasers: $SIVE $COHR $LITE
🔹 Switch platforms: $AVGO $NVDA
🔹 Optical engines / photonics: Lightmatter, Ayar Labs, $MRVL, Celestial, POET
🔹 Silicon photonics foundry: $TSM $GFS $TSEM
The name that stands out most to me is $SIVE.
At roughly a $1.5B market cap, SIVE is becoming increasingly important in the laser supply chain for CPO.
If Chinese laser suppliers remain behind the required CPO performance curve, Western laser leaders could become a major strategic bottleneck.

text:
Wavelength-scale nonlocal metadevices shatter finite-size Q limits, trapping light like an infinite crystal in a ring the size of a single wavelength while emitting pristine vector beams on demand. Researchers from Harbin Institute of Technology (Shenzhen), Pengcheng Laboratory, and collaborators fused bound states in the continuum (BICs) with topology to engineer nonlocal metasurfaces bent into closed, continuous-boundary rings. 

Traditional finite nonlocal metasurfaces bleed radiation at edges, capping quality factors. Seamless loops eliminate lateral radiative channels, yielding experimental Q factors approaching 10^5 at true wavelength scales and rivaling ideal 1D infinite quasi-BICs. This topological approach conquers the boundary curse. 

Strong meta-atom collective interactions suppress unwanted radiation while preserving controllable vertical emission. Multipole analysis shows the BIC mode dominated by magnetic dipole and electric quadrupole contributions, minimizing leakage unlike the leaky mode’s toroidal and quadrupole-heavy profile. Size-dependent tests confirm saturation at large radii matches the infinite-case limit, with robust fabrication tolerances against grating shifts, overlays, and height variations.

Dual vector beam generation, radial and azimuthal polarizations, plus OAM beams and nonlinear responses from one compact device. On-chip photonics gains high-Q, mode-multiplexing capability for ultra-efficient light trapping in sensors, lasers, quantum tech, and structured-light applications, all in smaller-than-conventional footprints.

Photonic chips can now confine light in whispering-gallery-like modes while emitting engineered vortices on cue, bridging classical confinement with nonlocal coherence. Like bending a leaky fence into an unbreakable Möbius loop, this seamless marriage of metasurface nonlocality and microcavity topology marks a record advance. Points toward scalable integrated photonics that defy size-Q trade-offs.

Like bending a leaky fence into an unbreakable Möbius loop, this seamless marriage of metasurface nonlocality and microcavity topology marks a record advance. Points toward scalable integrated photonics that defy size-Q trade-offs."}}}, "rest_id": "2076778095147950241", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "196", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Morgan Stanley’s latest CPO report validates my research:

Key CPO players include:

🔹 Laser supply chain: $SIVE, $COHR, $LITE

🔹 Switch platforms: $Broadcom, $Nvidia

🔹 Optical engines & photonics:
$Lightmatter, Ayar Labs, $Marvell/Celestial, POET

🔹 Silicon photonics foundry capacity: $TSM, $GFS, $TSEM

What stands out is $SIVE — a ~$1.5B laser company that is emerging as a critical player in the CPO ecosystem.
Combined with Rosenblatt’s view that Chinese laser suppliers are still far from meeting CPO requirements, the importance of the leading Western laser companies in this strategic bottleneck becomes even clearer.

CPO could become a major tech battleground ahead

$IQE

The ultimate AI photonics turnaround story is happening right now: $IQE (IQE plc) is quietly positioning itself at the epicentre of the global hardware super-cycle.

While retail investors obsess over overhyped silicon plays, this UK semiconductor giant has spent the year completely transforming its business. By providing the advanced compound semiconductor and epitaxial wafers that allow chips to transmit data via light rather than electricity, IQE is resolving the biggest physical speed bottlenecks in AI data centres.

Why the macro bull case is exceptionally strong right now:

🔹 The MACOM Endorsement: Secured a transformational £81 million strategic investment and long-term supply agreement with Nasdaq-listed titan ⁠MACOM Technology Solutions, completely wiping out previous balance sheet worries.

🔹 Explosive Revenue Guidance: Management is officially tracking toward >20% year-on-year revenue growth, backed by a massive influx of orders for their Indium Phosphide (InP) photonics solutions.

🔹 High-Margin Operating Leverage: As factory capacity utilization scales back up across the UK, US, and Taiwan, their adjusted EBITDA is projected to catapult into high-single to low-double digits.

🔹 Huge Analyst Price Targets: Major City institutions (like Deutsche Numis) have strongly backed the turnaround, initiating a 60p price target—representing a massive upside from its current consolidation range.

The massive inventory correction that plagued the industry is over, and the AI data centre boom has officially arrived. Don't miss the boat on this structural rebound.

text:
$SIVE

Don't let the recent pullback fool you—the smart money is aggressively buying the dip on $SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors).

While the broader market fixated on temporary dilution, the Sivers Board of Directors and CEO just dropped a massive vote of confidence by buying a wave of shares directly on the open market. Sivers isn't just riding the AI photonics wave; they are providing the foundational laser technology powering it.

Why the structural bull case is stronger than ever:

🔹 The Hyperscaler Pipeline: Supplying customized, high-performance laser arrays that replace slow copper wiring with high-speed light inside major AI data clusters.

🔹 Massive Operating Leverage: Its sales pipeline expanded exponentially to nearly $800 million, setting up explosive multi-year growth as Tier-1 partnerships scale.

🔹 U.S. Dual-Listing Catalyst: Sivers is actively pushing toward a U.S. Nasdaq dual-listing, which will open the floodgates to massive pools of Wall Street institutional capital.

🔹 Extreme Short Squeeze Potential: With heavy short interest betting against it during this temporary correction, any major contract announcement risks triggering an epic, forced buyback rally.

Insiders are locking up shares because they know what's coming next. This is a prime optical infrastructure player sitting on a generational tech breakthrough.

text:
I mapped the entire future economy. Nine layers, from bedrock to orbit → and every layer is investable.

🛰️ SPACE → the orbital economy: $ASTS $RKLB $PL
⚡ ENERGY & STORAGE → powering the buildout: $TE $EOSE $SPKL
🚁 AUTONOMY & DRONES → machines that operate themselves: $ONDS $AUR $MRLN $KRKNF
🤖 PHYSICAL AI → intelligence that acts: $OUST $AMBA $OSS
💡 PHOTONICS → light replaces copper: $CRDO $SIVEF $AAOI $LITE 
🏗️ AI INFRASTRUCTURE → the compute buildout: $IREN $NBIS $DGXX $WYFI 
🔬 GLASS & OPTICAL MATERIALS → the substrate light runs on: $GLW $LPTH $LPKF
📡 CONNECTIVITY & RF → moving the signal: $AMPG
⛏️ CRITICAL MINERALS → the hard constraints under everything: $MP $UAMY $ASPI

Here's what the map teaches that a watchlist can't: these aren't nine separate trades. They're one machine → the minerals feed the glass, the glass carries the light, the light feeds the compute, the compute powers the intelligence, the intelligence drives the machines, and the machines reach for orbit. Every layer is a chokepoint for the one above it.

Which is the entire investing philosophy in one image: don't chase the logo at the top of the stack → own the layers everything above is FORCED to buy from. Logos compete. Layers collect.

Important note: a map is a thesis, not a timing tool → layers de-rate together when the factor gets sold (see: this month), and not every name survives to see its layer win. Diversification across a stack is still one machine.

The future economy isn't a sector. It's a supply chain → and it's hiring.

Nine layers, from bedrock to orbit → and every layer is investable. 🛰️ SPACE → the orbital economy: $ASTS $RKLB $PL ⚡ ENERGY & STORAGE → powering the buildout: $TE $EOSE $SPKL 🚁 AUTONOMY & DRONES → machines that operate themselves: $ONDS $AUR $MRLN $KRKNF 🤖 PHYSICAL AI → intelligence that acts: $OUST $AMBA $OSS 💡 PHOTONICS → light replaces copper: $CRDO $SIVEF $AAOI $LITE 🏗️ AI INFRASTRUCTURE → the compute buildout: $IREN $NBIS $DGXX $WYFI 🔬 GLASS & OPTICAL MATERIALS → the substrate light runs on: $GLW $LPTH $LPKF 📡 CONNECTIVITY & RF → moving the signal: $AMPG ⛏️ CRITICAL MINERALS → the hard constraints under everything: $MP $UAMY $ASPI Here's what the map teaches that a watchlist can't: these aren't nine separate trades. They're one machine → the minerals feed the glass, the glass carries the light, the light feeds the compute, the compute powers the intelligence, the intelligence drives the machines, and the machines reach for orbit. Every layer is a chokepoint for the one above it. Which is the entire investing philosophy in one image: don't chase the logo at the top of the stack → own the layers everything above is FORCED to buy from. Logos compete. Layers collect. Important note: a map is a thesis, not a timing tool → layers de-rate together when the factor gets sold (see: this month), and not every name survives to see its layer win. Diversification across a stack is still one machine. The future economy isn't a sector. text:
$AEHR Those are valid risks, execution, customer concentration, and insider selling shouldn't be ignored. But the market is pricing the inflection, not the past. You're focusing on historical results, while the market is looking ahead. Stocks are priced on future expectations, not what happened last year. The key question isn't whether FY26 was weak; it's whether AI accelerator burn-in and silicon photonics become major production markets over the next 2-3 years. That's what investors are betting on. 
And the $10M of insider selling is peanuts in the context of a long-tenured founder's overall holdings, the more important question is whether they're abandoning the story, not whether they sold a fraction of their stake.

text:
$AEHR (Aehr Test Systems) just reported Q4 earnings after the close tonight — and the stock is surging around 36.87% to ~$93.30 on guidance that shocked even the most optimistic bulls.

Our scoring algorithm rated $AEHR 28 (Ugly) heading into today — the results proved why earnings surprises can override any score.

Here's every verified number:

Q4 FY2026 actual results:
→ Non-GAAP EPS: $0.11 — crushed -$0.01 estimate by $0.12 (1,200% beat) ✅
→ Revenue: $18.84M — beat $18.69M-$19.06M consensus, up 33% YoY ✅
→ GAAP net income: $1.4M — FIRST profitable quarter vs. -$2.9M a year ago ✅
→ Record Q4 bookings: $60.7M — a new all-time quarterly record ✅
→ Backlog (May 29): $80.6M ✅
→ Effective backlog (including post-quarter orders): $100.6M ✅
→ Cash: $116.5M — up from $37.1M just one quarter ago ✅

Full-year FY2026:
→ Revenue: $50.0M — down from $59.0M in FY2025 (the timing gap year)
→ Non-GAAP net income: $0.9M — back to profitability on adjusted basis

The number that's moving the stock — FY2027 guidance:
→ Revenue: $130M-$150M — midpoint $140M
→ That represents 160%-200% year-over-year growth
→ Non-GAAP net income: 18%-22% of revenue — implying $23.4M-$33M in profit
→ Wall Street was expecting ~$70M before tonight

CEO Gayn Erickson: "With strong momentum and a record backlog of over $100 million heading into fiscal year 2027, we have substantial visibility into projected fiscal 2027 revenue."

What's driving the demand explosion:
→ AI processors — package-level burn-in from lead hyperscale customer (follow-on orders)
→ Silicon photonics — follow-on orders for FOX-XP wafer-level systems (July 7, July 9)
→ Power semiconductors — $8M in new silicon carbide wafer contactor orders
→ Benchmark testing completed with a major AI accelerator supplier

The context that makes the beat even sweeter:
$AEHR had been down around 37% over the past month before today — deleted from the Russell Microcap Index on June 27. The stock had pulled from $126.62 to ~$68. Today's earnings give the bulls exactly what they needed: concrete $130M-$150M FY2027 guidance backed by a $100.6M effective backlog.

52-week range: $14.01-$126.62. YTD: up around 382% before tonight.
Institutional holders: T. Rowe Price added +3,824%. Goldman Sachs added +617%.

Guided $130M-$150M. Wall Street expected $70M. Up around 37% tonight.

I personally like Aehr more since no one else can do WLBI at that scale. Plus, optionality across other applications e.g. SiC, GaN, and photonics. All makes Aehr more compelling to me.

I did have positions in Form a while back though, they're probably a safer compounder since their rev lines seem a little more varied in other areas like hbm and dram customers. Vs. Aehr which does have high conc. in a couple customers currently (but are all buying more and more from them)

text:
"Hey Alva, why is $AEHR ripping 30% after hours? Should I still buy? Please analyze."

Because the market finally got what it wanted: AI revenue visibility. Let's discuss.

1/ Official Q4/FY26 numbers:
$AEHR guided FY27 revenue to $130M-$150M. Street was around $85M.
For context, FY26 revenue was only $50M.
That is 160%-200% growth next year.

2/ Why the stock is up:
1. Backlog gives visibility
$AEHR has ~$100.6M effective backlog against a $130M -$150M FY27 revenue guide. That means a large chunk of next year is already visible.

2. AI processor WLBI demand is accelerating
Management said the lead AI production customer is shifting burn-in from system-level to wafer-level for AI accelerators. This change in workflow can create recurring equipment + consumables demand.

3. Silicon photonics is becoming real
They now have a lead silicon photonics customer ramping and a newer major networking customer forecasting additional systems this year. This is the optical AI data center angle the market wants.

4. SiC is recovering
They got about $8M in new silicon carbide WaferPak orders over the last month, including from a top global automaker. That helps because AEHR was previously hurt by SiC weakness.

3/The risk: valuation. 
At around $93 after hours, $AEHR is roughly a $3.1B company. Against FY27 revenue guidance of      $130M-$150M, that's around 21x-24x forward sales. So it's quite expensive. The market is paying for multi-year AI/silicon-photonics optionality.

4/ Trading read
$AEHR stock moved from the low $70s pre-release to as high as about $96.8 after hours, last around $93.25. I would not blindly chase after a +30% move. Here's what I'd do
- If it holds $90-$92, the market is accepting the new guide. 
- Above $96-$97, momentum can keep squeezing. 
- If it loses $84-$85, the first reaction is fading and you probably get a better entry later.

5/ Alva's take
Overall, great earnings. The thesis is also confirmed, but its price already pulled a lot of the good news forward. For holders, this is a strong hold/trim-some-if-oversized print. For fresh buyers, I’d wait for the first pullback or a clean reclaim above the after-hours high.

text:
$AEHR beat market expectations by returning to profitability in the fourth quarter and provided a positive financial outlook for the upcoming fiscal year.

A primary driver of this market reaction is the company's order backlog, which now exceeds $100 million and secures a significant portion of its future revenue.

This growth is driven by increasing demand for wafer-level testing systems, which are used to identify semiconductor defects early in AI processors and silicon photonics for data centers.

Additionally, $AEHR completed technical evaluations with a major AI processor vendor, resulting in follow-on orders from large technology hyperscalers, alongside $8 million in new orders for silicon carbide applications.

Financially, the company strengthened its balance sheet, increasing its cash and cash equivalents to $116.5 million from $37.1 million in the previous quarter.

text:
$AEHR The beat and FY27 guidance are impressive.

But I think this sentence matters even more:

“We continue to work with multiple memory suppliers…”

That may be the clearest signal yet that WLBI is expanding beyond AI processors.

If burn-in moves into HBM, NAND, silicon photonics and advanced AI packages, Aehr’s addressable market changes completely.

This wasn’t just a strong quarter.

It was a roadmap update.

Long $AEHR

- Multiple customers entering/expanding production
- Huge backlog
- Opportunities in new markets e.g. NAND and HBM https://t.co/TcsnbaSq4V
