text:
$AEHR The AI Thesis Keeps Playing Out Annnd Now SiC Is Reviving Too!!

AEHR guided FY2027 revenue to $ 130-150M. That’s 160-200% YoY growth, backed by a record ~$100.6 M effective backlog, not optimism, but visibility. 

They also expect non-GAAP net income at 18-22% of revenue.

Q4 already showed the swing: $18.8M revenue (+33% YoY), turned profitable ($1.4M GAAP / $3.6M non-GAAP), record $60.7M in bookings, cash jumped to $116.5 M.

The AI engine is confirming and expanding:
• Lead production customer ramping wafer-level burn-in for AI accelerators (training + inference) a structural content increase per chip.

• New top-tier AI supplier benchmark win now moving to pilot on a second device.

• Record follow-on Sonoma orders from lead hyperscaler for next-gen higher-power packages.

• Silicon photonics emerging as a third leg (new global networking customer + systems for optical I/O & data-center interconnects).

• Memory roadmap targeting NAND and potential high-bandwidth memory burn-in.

At the same time, the SiC business the market wrote off is coming back:

• >$8M new SiC wafer-level burn-in orders in the past month, including a direct order from one of the world’s largest automakers + first SiC customer in Taiwan.

This is no longer AI replacing SiC. It’s AI compounding while SiC recovers as a second engine underneath. Diversified across AI accelerators, photonics, memory, data-center power, with the EV-driven SiC business finally turning up.

Strong setup for a small-cap semi name with this level of visibility. The real risk is concentration in the lead AI customer. 

Key questions on the call: how much of the backlog sits with that one customer, is the benchmark win a new logo, and how the backlog splits across AI / SiC / photonics.

text:
$AEHR The Guidance: Management officially projected FY2027 revenue to be between $130 million and $150 million.
 Why it matters: Wall Street analysts were only modeling ~$82 million. AEHR's actual forecast represents a staggering 160% to 200% year-over-year growth trajectory.
 Profitability Margin: They also guided that non-GAAP net income will landing at 18% to 22% of total revenue.
What This Tells Us
The massive record bookings ($60.7 million) and total effective backlog ($100.6 million) prove that their pivot into custom ASIC package-level burn-in for hyperscale AI customers and silicon photonics isn't just a pilot project—it's scaling into high-volume manufacturing exceptionally fast. The supply chain bottleneck for AI infrastructure testing is officially paying off for them.

text:
$AEHR is up 30% after hours. 

It's certainly been a bumpy ride for photonics, but I think this ER is extremely bullish for the sector.  Congrats to the 3000+ investors who own this stock with me on @joinautopilot via my Photonics Is Next portfolio.

When @KawzInvests and I published our photonics testing deep dive, $AEHR had just printed a $10.3M revenue quarter and the market called it a miss. We said to watch the bookings, not the revenue.

Q4 settled it:

- Revenue of $18.8M, up 33% YoY
- Record bookings of $60.7M in a single quarter
- Effective backlog doubled in 90 days
- Back to GAAP profitability

Then the bomb. FY27 revenue guidance of $130M to $150M. That's 160% to 200% growth against $50M this year.

The drivers are exactly what we wrote about:

- Their lead AI customer is shifting all burn-in from system level to wafer level for its accelerators

- A benchmark with a major supplier of AI accelerators and CPUs exceeded expectations. They're now moving toward pilot production and handed $AEHR a second device to evaluate

- The lead silicon photonics customer is ramping with follow-on orders, and their newest customer, a global networking leader, is forecasting additional systems this year.

Remember the structural setup. Every silicon photonics wafer needs burn-in before packaging. $AEHR is still the only company doing this at production scale.

Everyone staring at 60x trailing sales missed that the guide compresses it to ~21x forward.

Super excited for photonics earnings season!

text:
$AEHR Q4 Performance & FY27 Outlook

​Financials

• ​Q4 Revenue: $19M (In-line).
• ​Q4 EPS: $0.11 (Beat vs. $0.01 loss estimate).

• ​Backlog: Strong position at $100M with $61M in new bookings.

• ​FY27 Guidance: $140M revenue (+180% YoY), significantly exceeding the $85M consensus.

​Key Drivers

• ​Silicon Photonics: Expanded to two customers, including a global networking leader, as AI data centers adopt optical I/O.

• ​Silicon Carbide: Added $8M in recent WaferPak orders, headlined by a top-three global automaker.

• ​Hyperscale (Sonoma): Record follow-on orders with a shift toward higher-power density devices.

high-speed optics. ​These are the key players building the essential layers of the AI optical stack: $AAOI - Transceiver Layer Dominating with vertically integrated U.S.-based InP laser manufacturing. Already landed a major 1.6T order ($200M+) from a leading hyperscaler, plus strong 800G follow-on business. $CRDO - Connectivity Layer Powering high-speed links via AECs and advanced silicon. The DustPhotonics acquisition brings silicon photonics in-house, a massive edge as copper constraints intensify. $LITE - Laser & Switching Leader in EMLs and optical circuit switching. Backed by a $2B strategic investment from $NVDA with an optical switch backlog stretching into 2028. $COHR - InP Infrastructure Addressing the critical Indium Phosphide bottleneck. Holds a $2B $NVDA commitment and is scaling capacity aggressively for 1.6T+ networking. $CIEN - Transport Layer The backbone that ties everything together. Their solutions enable hyperscalers to move enormous AI traffic between data centers as clusters scale to gigawatt levels. $FN - Manufacturing Backbone The "picks-and-shovels" partner. As the reliable assembly giant for optical modules, they fuel the entire ramp where speed and scale determine the winners. $MRVL - DSP & Electro-Optics Strengthening the infrastructure with PAM DSPs and custom chips. The Celestial AI deal and NVLink exposure place them at the heart of next-gen cluster design. $AEHR - Testing & Reliability Providing essential wafer-level burn-in (FOX-XP). Recently secured a major leader in networking for 1.6T production ramp (critical for hyperscale reliability). The transition from copper is no longer optional 😬 Anyway these stocks are volatile, with risks around customer concentration, production ramps (InP/SiPho), and high valuations. DYOR. text:
$AEHR delivers: Record $60.7M Q4 bookings, $100M + effective backlog, and FY2027 outlook of $130 - 150M — 2.6–3.0x fiscal 2026 revenue. Strong execution in AI processors and silicon photonics positions the company at the intersection of wafer-level test innovation and hyperscale infrastructure demand.

A name included in the Photonics basket , +32% aftermarket. Structural tailwinds building. 
$AEHR

Aehr Test Systems ($AEHR) — Ρεκόρ παραγγελιών, ισχυρό backlog και αισιόδοξο FY27 outlook

📊 Αποτελέσματα

Έσοδα: $18,8 εκατ. (+33,3% σε ετήσια βάση)
GAAP EPS: $0,04, έναντι -$0,10 πέρυσι
Non-GAAP EPS: $0,11, έναντι -$0,01 πέρυσι
Ρεκόρ παραγγελιών (Bookings): $60,7 εκατ.
Effective Backlog: $100,6 εκατ.

🎯 Guidance (FY27)

FY27 Revenue: $130M–$150M
Expected Revenue Growth: +160% έως +200% σε ετήσια βάση
FY27 Non-GAAP Net Income: 18%–22% του revenue

📌 Βασικά σημεία

Η ζήτηση για AI processors επιταχύνεται τόσο στο wafer-level burn-in όσο και στο package-level burn-in.
Μεγάλος προμηθευτής AI processors ολοκλήρωσε benchmark testing και εξετάζει την έναρξη πιλοτικής παραγωγής.
Πελάτες στον χώρο του silicon photonics αυξάνουν την παραγωγική τους δυναμικότητα για εφαρμογές AI optical interconnects.
Η Aehr έλαβε περίπου $8 εκατ. νέες παραγγελίες για silicon carbide μέσα στον τελευταίο μήνα.
Τα διαθέσιμα μετρητά και τα restricted cash αυξήθηκαν στα $116,5 εκατ.

💬 Σχόλιο διοίκησης

«Με πολλούς πελάτες να εισέρχονται ή να επεκτείνουν την παραγωγή τους, με ρεκόρ backlog και με πρόσθετες ευκαιρίες τόσο στο wafer-level όσο και στο package-level burn-in, πιστεύουμε ότι η Aehr είναι τοποθετημένη για αρκετά χρόνια ισχυρής ανάπτυξης εσόδων.»

Remember when space stocks went down 50%, then bottomed for a while, then $ASTS won J-LEO and $RKLB acquired $IRDM.

And the entire space sector exploded upwards in a few days time.

Photonics has a similar setup here in my opinion.

Let's see tomorrow

text:
$AEHR just guided FY2027 revenue to $130M - $150M, up 160% to 200% from the $50M it printed in FY2026. 

Q4 came in at $18.8M with $0.11 adjusted EPS against a consensus loss, record bookings of $60.7M and an effective backlog of $100.6M 

The driver is silicon photonics burn in finally hitting production scale. Aehr’s lead SiPho customer keeps placing follow on orders for fully automated wafer level burn in systems, and a second major networking customer is ramping capacity. 

Every 800G/1.6T transceiver and optical I/O chip going into AI data centers needs reliability screening, and Aehr owns that step of the process.

This is the test layer of the optical interconnect buildout monetizing. Transceiver volume growth flows straight through to burn in capacity demand, and with AI processors and power semis stacked on top, the backlog gives unusual visibility for a company this size.

Stock seems to like it too

Up 28% so far.

Rosenblatt มองหุ้น Optical อ่อนตัวชั่วคราว ชี้ Short Seller เองยังไม่มั่นใจใน Thesis

Rosenblatt ออกมาวิเคราะห์ความอ่อนแอของกลุ่ม Optical / Photonics ในช่วง 1–2 เดือนที่ผ่านมา โดยมองว่าแรงขายส่วนหนึ่งเกิดจากความกลัวระยะสั้น ไม่ใช่การเปลี่ยนแปลงของปัจจัยพื้นฐาน

หุ้นที่ได้รับผลกระทบ เช่น
$AAOI
$LITE
$COHR
กลุ่ม CPO / Optical Networking อื่นๆ

สาเหตุที่หุ้น Optical ถูกขาย

Rosenblatt ระบุว่า ตลาดกังวลหลักจาก 2 ประเด็น

➤ รายงานว่า CPO (Co-Packaged Optics) อาจเลื่อนออกไป

➤ ความกังวลว่า จีนจะเพิ่มกำลังผลิตจนกดดันราคาเลเซอร์

โดยเฉพาะเทคโนโลยีที่ใช้ Indium Phosphide (InP) ซึ่งเป็นส่วนสำคัญของเลเซอร์สำหรับระบบ Optical

Short Seller เริ่มลดสถานะ?

Rosenblatt ระบุว่า

➤ พวกเขามองว่า Short Seller หลายราย ไม่ได้เชื่อมั่นในมุมมองขาลงอย่างจริงจัง

➤ จากการพูดคุยกับ Short Seller หลายราย พบว่าบางส่วนมีแผนปิดสถานะ Short ในช่วง

ปลายเดือนกรกฎาคม
ต้นเดือนสิงหาคม

และบางรายระบุว่าตั้งใจกลับมาซื้อหุ้น Optical ในปี 2027 เพื่อรับโอกาสจากการขยายตัวของ CPO Scale-up

"Stocks in the Optical sector have traded poorly for the last 1-2 months" due to CPO delay reports and China capacity scares.

"We think short sellers... do not actually strongly believe in the thesis."

text:
Rosenblatt on the recent optical weakness:

They think it’s more sentiment than fundamentals.

Some short sellers reportedly plan to cover in late July/early August and buy back ahead of the 2027 CPO ramp.

The photonics thesis is still alive.

But yeah, it will go to 0.

$SIVE $AAOI $LITE

text:
Everyone thinks AI is limited by chips.

It isn't.

The biggest bottleneck today is moving data between them.

An NVIDIA GPU can perform trillions of calculations every second.

But much of that time is spent waiting for information to arrive from other chips. As AI clusters grow to tens of thousands of GPUs, the connections between them are becoming the real limitation, not the compute itself.

Lightmatter looked at this problem and asked a different question.

What if computers stopped moving data with electricity...

...and started moving it with light?

Instead of electrons traveling through copper traces, Lightmatter uses silicon photonics to send information as photons.

Light can carry more data, travel farther with lower energy loss, and multiple signals can share the same optical fiber at once.

The crazy part is that they're not trying to build a faster GPU.

They're trying to change how every GPU talks to every other GPU.

It's like widening every highway in a city instead of making individual cars faster.

That's why companies building frontier AI systems are paying attention.

The next leap in AI performance might not come from a more powerful chip.

It might come from changing the language chips use to communicate.

Sometimes the biggest breakthrough isn't making the engine faster.

It's replacing the roads.

It isn't. The biggest bottleneck today is moving data between them. An NVIDIA GPU can perform trillions of calculations every second. But much of that time is spent waiting for information to arrive from other chips. As AI clusters grow to tens of thousands of GPUs, the connections between them are becoming the real limitation, not the compute itself. Lightmatter looked at this problem and asked a different question. What if computers stopped moving data with electricity... ...and started moving it with light? Instead of electrons traveling through copper traces, Lightmatter uses silicon photonics to send information as photons. Light can carry more data, travel farther with lower energy loss, and multiple signals can share the same optical fiber at once. The crazy part is that they're not trying to build a faster GPU. They're trying to change how every GPU talks to every other GPU. It's like widening every highway in a city instead of making individual cars faster. That's why companies building frontier AI systems are paying attention. The next leap in AI performance might not come from a more powerful chip. It might come from changing the language chips use to communicate. Sometimes the biggest breakthrough isn't making the engine faster. Almost nobody is asking where all of it will live. Biomemory thinks the answer isn't another hard drive. It's DNA (Actual synthetic DNA) The company is building storage systems where digital files are encoded into DNA molecules that can sit on a shelf for decades without needing electricity. Their target isn't your laptop. It's the cold archives inside data centers. The numbers are what caught my attention. They claim up to 150 years of data retention. No spinning disks. No SSD refresh cycles every few years. Just molecular storage designed for information that may never be accessed, but can never be lost. What's interesting is the timing. Data centers already consume enormous amounts of power, and the amount of data we generate keeps doubling. Instead of asking, "How do we build bigger servers?" Biomemory is asking, "What if we change the storage medium entirely?" It sounds like science fiction. Then you realize hard drives would've sounded the same 50 years ago. The real question isn't whether DNA will replace SSDs. It's whether, 20 years from now, every hyperscale data center quietly has a rack full of DNA sitting in the corner. I wouldn't bet against molecules."}}}, "rest_id": "2074418345534013760", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "118", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2075630757679382963", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "141", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Goldman Sachs is projecting a 10x explosion in AI networking infrastructure from $15 billion in 2026 to $154 billion by 2028 (Save this). 

As AI clusters scale from single racks to multi rack SuperPODs, the networking requirements change dramatically. 

The current GB300 NVL72 architecture is a $15 billion TAM today and when you move to the Rubin Ultra NVL576 SuperPOD configuration and the TAM explodes to $154 billion, a 10x increase in a single architectural transition.  

The key shift is that copper, which dominates scale-up networking inside today's racks, gives way to co-packaged optics at scale. 

CPO alone is a $91 billion opportunity at scale but here is the thing though Copper does not disappear.

In the NVL576 SuperPOD, copper backplane and flyover cables still account for $30.7 billion in TAM.  

Copper remains irreplaceable for ultra-short distances inside the chassis, the physics simply haven't changed and that is the insight that makes Marvell so interesting right now.

Marvell is the most underrated name in the entire AI infrastructure stack.

The company is a dominant supplier of the copper switching, backplane and SerDes technology that lives inside every major AI rack today but that is only the beginning of the thesis. 

Marvell also designs the custom AI ASICs, the XPUs that Google uses in TPUs, Amazon uses in Trainium, and Meta uses in its inference chips.  

They are the arms dealer to all three of the largest hyperscalers in the world simultaneously and the numbers are starting to reflect what insiders already knew.

Marvell posted record fiscal year 2026 revenue of $8.195 billion, up 42% year over year and they guided for FY27 revenue approaching $11 billion and FY28 approaching $15 billion.  

Data center revenue now represents 76% of total sales, and the company has over 50 custom AI ASIC design wins entering production in FY2028-2029. 

Marvell also owns Celestial AI's photonic fabric platform connects AI processors and memory using light instead of copper, meaning Marvell owns the copper layer today and is building the optical layer for tomorrow.  

Most people look at Marvell and see a company that has already run but what they are missing is that the actual revenue from those 50+ design wins hasn't even started ramping yet.

Extremely bullish on Marvell and make sure to follow me @MelvinInvests for more AI infrastructure ideas.

As AI clusters scale from single racks to multi rack SuperPODs, the networking requirements change dramatically. The current GB300 NVL72 architecture is a $15 billion TAM today and when you move to the Rubin Ultra NVL576 SuperPOD configuration and the TAM explodes to $154 billion, a 10x increase in a single architectural transition. The key shift is that copper, which dominates scale-up networking inside today's racks, gives way to co-packaged optics at scale. CPO alone is a $91 billion opportunity at scale but here is the thing though Copper does not disappear. In the NVL576 SuperPOD, copper backplane and flyover cables still account for $30.7 billion in TAM. Copper remains irreplaceable for ultra-short distances inside the chassis, the physics simply haven't changed and that is the insight that makes Marvell so interesting right now. Marvell is the most underrated name in the entire AI infrastructure stack. The company is a dominant supplier of the copper switching, backplane and SerDes technology that lives inside every major AI rack today but that is only the beginning of the thesis. Marvell also designs the custom AI ASICs, the XPUs that Google uses in TPUs, Amazon uses in Trainium, and Meta uses in its inference chips. They are the arms dealer to all three of the largest hyperscalers in the world simultaneously and the numbers are starting to reflect what insiders already knew. Marvell posted record fiscal year 2026 revenue of $8.195 billion, up 42% year over year and they guided for FY27 revenue approaching $11 billion and FY28 approaching $15 billion. Data center revenue now represents 76% of total sales, and the company has over 50 custom AI ASIC design wins entering production in FY2028-2029. Marvell also owns Celestial AI's photonic fabric platform connects AI processors and memory using light instead of copper, meaning Marvell owns the copper layer today and is building the optical layer for tomorrow. Most people look at Marvell and see a company that has already run but what they are missing is that the actual revenue from those 50+ design wins hasn't even started ramping yet. text:
Rosenblatt Says the Optical Thesis Is Still Intact

• The photonics selloff is being driven by fear, not fundamentals
• Even with more Chinese InP capacity, laser demand still exceeds supply through 2030
• The "CPO delay" is really just a slower volume ramp, with NPO filling much of the 2027 demand
• Many short sellers reportedly plan to cover before earnings and buy back into the sector

They also expect $AAOI to beat and raise, with Q2 revenue above $200M and Q3 guiding toward ~$300M

The longterm optical demand story hasn't changed
Only a matter of time ...

Thanks for pointing this out @michaelsikand 

$LITE $COHR $AAOI $SIVE

text:
Rosenblatt: Photonics short sellers have zero conviction and are preying on fear.

They point out that short sellers have no real conviction in the short thesis and actually intend to buy the stocks back after covering before these companies smash earnings.

Optical stocks have traded down for 1-2 months on two stories: fears that Chinese indium phosphide capacity crashes laser pricing for $LITE $COHR $AAOI, and reports that scale up CPO slips a year. 

Rosenblatt figures Chinese players add 20-40% to their 2030 InP forecast, but they already model 2030 capacity running 40% behind demand. So even with new supply the industry is still short lasers. 

They also argue the CPO "delay" is overblown. The process isn't delayed, only the ramp to volume. 2028 is now the inflection year and NPO fills the 2027 gap with nearly identical laser needs. Same photons, same suppliers, same thesis.

You might see photonics red right now but the smart money is setting up to reload here ahead of earnings.

$AAOI has been hit particularly hard and it's well known Rosenblatt has the highest street PT. So it's nice to see a bullish note here ahead of ER.

"We expect AAOI to beat and raise and for 2Q26 to mark a watershed quarter where this starts happening consistently. We believe 2Q26 revenues can beat, and be above $200mn, with 800G sales to Amazon and Oracle as the primary driver of upside. The 3Q26 guidance should be ~$300mn with better GMs versus the ~30% level in 1H26"

Can't wait to see photonics explode back soon.

$LITE $COHR $AAOI $CIEN $VIAV $AXTI

We're kinda bearish CPO. $APH is going to perform much better over the next few years than previously expected.” Full quote: “The spending associated with AI chips is currently at or below 10%, but when we move to CPO, networking will grow even further, to 20% or 30%. We are seeing a huge uplift in networking content. CPO is a massive step-function change for the industry, and everyone recognizes it now, but people are getting a little too exuberant. In my view, it is not coming in 2027. It will really arrive toward the end of 2028, with 2029 being the real ramp for scale-up CPO. There have been a lot of manufacturing problems. If we could deploy it today at a good cost, everyone would do it. However, it is really hard. The manufacturing volumes and yields are not there, and the chips are not fully designed for it yet. It is a very complex and difficult technology to ramp, so people are going to stay with copper for as long as they can. That means Rubin is all copper, and Feynman, the next-generation Nvidia GPU after Rubin and Rubin Ultra, is still copper. We are not even shipping Rubin yet. We have a few generations of chips before we get to CPO on the GPU. CPO on switches is coming earlier than on GPUs or AI ASICs. Even without CPO, as cluster sizes get bigger, you need more optics, active electrical cables, and similar components per GPU. We have seen a major shift in the dynamics. On Monday, we released a note for our institutional research subscribers stating that, over the medium term, we are actually very bullish on copper and non-CPO optics, while being slightly bearish on CPO. This is due to delays in downstream chips, such as Feynman not fully utilizing CPO. Copper companies like Amphenol, which make backplane connectors and cables, are going to perform much better over the next few years than previously expected because CPO is being delayed. Ultimately, integrating optics is much more expensive than sending signals electrically. However, electrical signals cannot travel very far without adding repeaters or optics. There is a trade-off continuum, and while CPO will happen, it is currently being pushed out.”"}}}, "rest_id": "2075874882651553984", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "113744", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2075947753314931094", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "2608", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
NEW ALL IN... kidding but

Added $SIVEF / $SIVE at $4.66 two days ago, sitting at $5 now. Nice little unrealized +6.7%

This is NOT a core position like $PENG or $DRAM in the book. Sized small, short-term, purely tactical and that's why I am Positioned accordingly.

The thesis: CPO/photonics names got way too oversold across the board. SIVE is InP-based photonics, positioned for 1.6T and CPO, with a real Nasdaq NY dual-listing catalyst in play. Relatively speculative, thin float, no volume revenue yet to back the multiple, and one thing to point out, $SIVE started tp snap back hard off oversold levels but the other highly speculative names haven't $AAOI $AXTI, and that strength is important.

Not a "this is the next PENG" call. It's a bounce trade on a name with a legit long-term option attached if the LiDAR ramp and uplisting land later this year. Positioned accordingly, even that if it round-trips, it doesn't matter.

Real-time sizing and trims posted in the Inner Circle Discord, it's in bio.

NFA

text:
$HSAI operates on a completely different scale and cost curve than Western peers like Ouster. While regulatory moats like the SAFE LiDAR Act protect US domestic production, they do not resolve uncompetitive Western cost structures.

Hesai leverages millions of units in volume -- achieving a commanding 43% global ADAS market share with dominant EV players like Xiaomi and Li Auto -- to fund continuous, chip-level R&D. By integrating complex components onto proprietary Silicon Photonics  and SoC architectures, Hesai has successfully driven unit costs down to an unmatched $150 -- $200 benchmark while maintaining strong 40% gross margins and positive net income. Furthermore, its massive expansion into global Level 3 autonomy (e.g., Mercedes-Benz) and the explosive physical AI/robotics market (partner with Nvidia) ensures robust growth entirely independent of US automotive procurement.

Now the "scary" headline this replaced:

Earlier this week: "Head of Sivers Photonics dumped his ENTIRE stake."
Primary sources: Andrew McKee was moved from MD Photonics to a technology role early this year. Alex McCann has been "Managing Director for the photonics business" in Sivers' own releases since March 11, 2026.
McKee wasn't the division head. And per reported data, his early sales went out under 12 SEK before the run. That's not an informed insider selling the top. That's a 27-year veteran winding down toward retirement.

Who amplified the misleading "sold all his shares" headline first?

Ningi Research. Anonymous short seller. Profits when $SIVE falls. Previously flagged by Germany's BaFin for anonymous investment advice. Their last big Swedish target, Sinch, finished UP 17% after rebuttal.

text:
Hybrid quantum-classical de novo design of MHC-binding peptides

1. The study presents an end-to-end hybrid quantum-classical pipeline for de novo design of MHC class I binding peptides, using a real photonic quantum processor to generate latent vectors that seed a conditional GAN.

2. Core idea: the choice of latent “prior” distribution is treated as a controllable inductive bias. Instead of the default factorized Gaussian noise, the model uses correlated, non-classical priors sampled via Gaussian Boson Sampling (GBS) on a 32-mode photonic device.

3. Experimental setup: a conditional WGAN-GP is trained on 105,970 mass-spec-validated 9-mer HLA-I ligands (about 77k unique peptides) spanning 126 HLA alleles; the generator is conditioned on HLA labels to learn allele-specific binding constraints.

4. Priors compared at the same latent dimension (32): classical Gaussian and Bernoulli baselines vs (i) simulated GBS quantum prior and (ii) hardware-measured GBS prior. Quantum-derived priors are rescaled to zero mean/unit variance per dimension to avoid trivial distribution-mismatch effects.

5. In silico evaluation across 131 alleles (including 5 not in training): the trained generator produces 1000 peptides per allele, and NetMHCpan-4.2 EL %rank is used to call strong binders (threshold 0.5% rank). Across 30 random seeds, quantum priors increase predicted strong-binder yield and reduce variability.

6. The improvement is not uniform: gains are concentrated in alleles where classical priors perform worst (data-sparse/understudied alleles). A per-allele linear mixed model reports an average improvement vs Gaussian of +10.6 strong binders per 1000 (simulated quantum, p=0.001) and +6.3 per 1000 (real quantum, p=0.049).

7. Mechanistic signal: for three selected understudied alleles (HLA-A*31:01, HLA-A*68:01, HLA-B*37:01), quantum priors lead to broader exploration at non-anchor positions (higher positional Shannon entropy) while preserving anchor specificity (e.g., basic C-terminus for A*68:01; acidic P2 for B*37:01).

8. In vitro validation: for each of the three alleles, the top 20 quantum-prior peptides by predicted EL %rank are synthesized and tested using UV-mediated peptide exchange followed by pMHC stability ELISA. Quantum-designed peptides robustly stabilize pMHC complexes; negative-control low-predicted binders fail, and the strongest single binders observed per allele come from the quantum-prior model in the reported comparison set.

9. Scope and positioning: the authors explicitly do not claim quantum advantage at this scale (32 modes is classically simulable), but argue that hardware-realisable, structured non-classical priors can be a practical inductive bias for peptide generative modeling, especially for rare alleles relevant to personalized vaccines and T-cell therapies.

The study presents an end-to-end hybrid quantum-classical pipeline for de novo design of MHC class I binding peptides, using a real photonic quantum processor to generate latent vectors that seed a conditional GAN. 2. Core idea: the choice of latent “prior” distribution is treated as a controllable inductive bias. Instead of the default factorized Gaussian noise, the model uses correlated, non-classical priors sampled via Gaussian Boson Sampling (GBS) on a 32-mode photonic device. 3. Experimental setup: a conditional WGAN-GP is trained on 105,970 mass-spec-validated 9-mer HLA-I ligands (about 77k unique peptides) spanning 126 HLA alleles; the generator is conditioned on HLA labels to learn allele-specific binding constraints. 4. Priors compared at the same latent dimension (32): classical Gaussian and Bernoulli baselines vs (i) simulated GBS quantum prior and (ii) hardware-measured GBS prior. Quantum-derived priors are rescaled to zero mean/unit variance per dimension to avoid trivial distribution-mismatch effects. 5. In silico evaluation across 131 alleles (including 5 not in training): the trained generator produces 1000 peptides per allele, and NetMHCpan-4.2 EL %rank is used to call strong binders (threshold 0.5% rank). Across 30 random seeds, quantum priors increase predicted strong-binder yield and reduce variability. 6. The improvement is not uniform: gains are concentrated in alleles where classical priors perform worst (data-sparse/understudied alleles). A per-allele linear mixed model reports an average improvement vs Gaussian of +10.6 strong binders per 1000 (simulated quantum, p=0.001) and +6.3 per 1000 (real quantum, p=0.049). 7. Mechanistic signal: for three selected understudied alleles (HLA-A*31:01, HLA-A*68:01, HLA-B*37:01), quantum priors lead to broader exploration at non-anchor positions (higher positional Shannon entropy) while preserving anchor specificity (e.g., basic C-terminus for A*68:01; acidic P2 for B*37:01). 8. In vitro validation: for each of the three alleles, the top 20 quantum-prior peptides by predicted EL %rank are synthesized and tested using UV-mediated peptide exchange followed by pMHC stability ELISA. Quantum-designed peptides robustly stabilize pMHC complexes; negative-control low-predicted binders fail, and the strongest single binders observed per allele come from the quantum-prior model in the reported comparison set. 9. Scope and positioning: the authors explicitly do not claim quantum advantage at this scale (32 modes is classically simulable), but argue that hardware-realisable, structured non-classical priors can be a practical inductive bias for peptide generative modeling, especially for rare alleles relevant to personalized vaccines and T-cell therapies. 💻Code: https://t.co/Dd9SRb1QIL 📜Paper: https://t.co/sVD0O7Hm6s #ComputationalBiology #Immunoinformatics #MHC #PeptideDesign #GenerativeModels #GAN #QuantumComputing #PhotonicQuantum #HybridAI #VaccineDesign #Neoantigens"}}}, "rest_id": "2075929388613550437", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "5734", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
AI 瓶頸議題

- 電力瓶頸（Power Bottleneck）
類比半導體的時代來臨：
摩爾定律不適用的類比領域價值正爆炸性成長，讓供應商擁有壓倒性的定價能力。主要廠商如 TI、NXP、Infineon 已在 2026 年同步執行漲價，證明了這一供應商主導的市場格局。

12,500 安培與 800V 直流的物理極限：
在 600kW 至 1MW 的超高密度 AI 機架中，若繼續使用傳統 48V 供電，電流將暴增至 12,500A，熱損耗將使運作變得不可能。因此，將架構轉移至 800V 以將電流降至 750A，並減少 45% 銅材用量，是物理上的必然選擇。

垂直供電（Vertical Power Delivery, VPD）：
為了避免在「最後一吋」降壓至 GPU 核心電壓（約 0.7V）時產生損耗與電壓波動，VPD——可謂「電源世界的 CPO」——正快速興起，將電源轉換器直接置於 GPU 晶片下方。

- 訊號與光學瓶頸（Signal & Photonics Bottleneck）
依距離重新分配銅線與光學：
「光學將完全取代銅線」的簡化說法並不正確。實際上是依距離進行重新分配：銅線與類比元件負責短距離（scale-up 領域），光學則接管長距離（機架間連接）。

Semtech 的類比魔法：
在 200G/lane 高速通訊中，Semtech 的技術備受關注——它完全省略了耗電高且造成延遲的 DSP，僅使用類比等化器（Analog Equalizer）來修正訊號，將功耗降低十倍，並把延遲從奈秒（ns）大幅縮減至皮秒（ps）。

光學技術真正的瓶頸在「光源」：
雖然 CPO是長期正確方向，但未來 2~3 年現實的贏家仍是 Near-Packaged Optics（NPO）與 1.6T 可插拔模組。光通訊真正的瓶頸位於成品光學模組之下：InP基板、CW雷射光源、由 Soitec 壟斷的 Photonics-SOI 晶圓，以及 SiPho 代工廠。

網路可靠度與「Link Flap」現象：
光學連結瞬間閃斷的「Link Flap」現象，會打斷整個 AI 叢集的同步運算（all-reduce），導致 GPU 大量閒置。在大型叢集中，能保證可靠度而非僅追求頻寬的解決方案（如 Credo 的 Zero-Flap），成為決定經濟可行性的終極瓶頸。

Power Bottleneck

> The Moment for Analog Semiconductors: The value of the analog domain, where Moore's Law does not apply, is exploding, giving suppliers overwhelming pricing power. Major players like TI, NXP, and Infineon are proving this dominant supplier market by executing synchronized price hikes in 2026.

> The Physics of 12,500 Amperes and 800V DC: In ultra-high-density AI racks of 600kW to 1MW, sticking to traditional 48V power distribution causes the current to skyrocket to 12,500A, making operations impossible due to heat loss. Therefore, shifting the architecture to 800V to reduce the current to 750A and cut copper requirements by 45% is a physical inevitability.

> Vertical Power Delivery (VPD): To prevent loss and voltage fluctuations during the "last inch" of stepping down to the GPU core voltage (around 0.7V), VPD—essentially the "CPO of the power world"—is emerging, which places the power converter directly beneath the GPU die.

Signal & Photonics Bottleneck

> Distance-Based Redistribution of Copper and Optics: The simplistic narrative that "optics will completely wipe out copper" is incorrect. A distance-based redistribution is intensifying, where copper and analog handle short distances (the scale-up domain) and optics take over long distances (rack-to-rack connectivity).

> Semtech’s Analog Magic: In 200G/lane high-speed communication, Semtech's technology is garnering massive attention by completely eliminating DSPs (Digital Signal Processors)—which consume heavy power and cause latency—and correcting signals using only Analog Equalizers. This reduces power consumption by tenfold and slashes latency from nanoseconds (ns) to picoseconds (ps).

> The Real Bottleneck in Optical Tech is the 'Light Source': While Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) is the correct long-term direction, the realistic winners for the next 2 to 3 years are Near-Packaged Optics (NPO) and 1.6T pluggable modules. The true bottlenecks in optical communication lie below the finished optical module layer: InP (Indium Phosphide) substrates, Continuous Wave (CW) laser sources, Photonics-SOI wafers (monopolized by Soitec), and SiPho foundries.

> Network Reliability and 'Link Flap': The "Link Flap" phenomenon, where an optical link momentarily blinks, disrupts the synchronization (all-reduce) of the entire AI cluster and leaves GPUs idling. In massive clusters, solutions that guarantee reliability rather than just bandwidth (such as Credo's Zero-Flap) become the ultimate bottleneck determining economic viability.

text:
FOTONİK ŞİRKETLERİ NASIL PARA KAZANIYOR?

Yapay zekâ yatırımları konuşulunca herkes GPU’lara odaklanıyor. Ancak binlerce işlemcinin aynı anda çalışabilmesi için verinin sunucular ve veri merkezleri arasında çok hızlı taşınması gerekiyor.

İşte fotonik şirketleri tam olarak burada devreye giriyor.

Elektrik sinyallerini ışığa dönüştürüyor, veriyi fiber kablolar üzerinden taşıyor ve karşı tarafta yeniden elektrik sinyaline çeviriyorlar.

✅ Applied Optoelectronics $AAOI

$AAOI daha çok ürünün son kullanıcıya yakın tarafında bulunuyor. Şirket; lazer çipleri, optik alt bileşenler, yüksek hızlı optik transceiver’lar ve kablolu internet altyapısında kullanılan HFC ürünleri üretiyor.

Veri merkezleri için sattığı transceiver’lar, sunucular ve ağ cihazları arasındaki elektrik sinyalini ışığa çevirerek fiber üzerinden taşıyor.

Şirketin gelir modeli oldukça basit: Bir hyperscaler yeni bir transceiver’ı test ediyor, ürünü onaylıyor ve ardından yüksek adetli sipariş vermeye başlıyor.

400G’den 800G’ye, ardından 1.6T bağlantılara geçiş hızlandıkça satılan ürünlerin kapasitesi ve üretim hacmi artıyor.

$AAOI ayrıca kablolu internet operatörlerine ağ ekipmanı sattığı için yalnızca AI veri merkezlerine bağımlı değil. 2025 yılında gelirlerinin yaklaşık %53,8’i CATV, %42,9’u ise veri merkezi pazarından geldi.

✅ Lumentum $LITE

$LITE fotonik ekosisteminin daha geniş ve daha çeşitlendirilmiş oyuncularından biri. Şirket yalnızca tamamlanmış optik modüller değil; lazerler, fotonik çipler, optik bileşenler, modüller ve ağ alt sistemleri satıyor.

Müşterileri arasında veri merkezi işletmecileri, telekomünikasyon ekipmanı üreticileri ve ağ altyapısı şirketleri bulunuyor.

$LITE ayrıca yarı iletken üretimi, batarya üretimi, metal kesme, kaynak ve 3D algılama gibi alanlarda kullanılan endüstriyel lazerlerden de gelir elde ediyor. Yani $LITE yalnızca AI veri merkezi yatırımlarına değil, çok daha geniş bir lazer ve optik teknoloji ekosistemine yatırım sunuyor.

✅ Sivers Semiconductors $SIVE

$SIVE diğer iki şirkete göre daha küçük, daha erken aşamada ve teknolojinin daha özel bir bölümünde yer alıyor. Photonics tarafında AI veri merkezleri için yüksek güçlü DFB lazerler ve lazer dizileri geliştiriyor.

Bu lazerler özellikle co-packaged optics sistemlerinde kullanılabilir. Bu teknolojide optik bağlantılar işlemcilere mümkün olduğunca yaklaştırılarak veri aktarımında enerji tüketiminin azaltılması hedefleniyor.

Şirket ayrıca LiDAR, robotik, üç boyutlu görüntüleme ve biyosensör uygulamalarına yönelik lazerler de geliştiriyor.

$SIVE’nin ikinci faaliyet alanı ise kablosuz iletişim.

Şirket; uydu iletişimi, sabit kablosuz erişim ve savunma sistemleri için mmWave çipleri, anten modülleri ve beamformer ürünleri üretiyor.

Photonics bölümünde gelir ağırlıklı olarak lazer donanımı satışından geliyor. Wireless bölümünde ise şirket hem donanım satıyor hem de müşteriye özel ürün geliştirme sözleşmelerinden gelir elde ediyor.

Şirket ayrıca LiDAR, robotik, üç boyutlu görüntüleme ve biyosensör uygulamalarına yönelik lazerler de geliştiriyor. $SIVE’nin ikinci faaliyet alanı ise kablosuz iletişim. Şirket; uydu iletişimi, sabit kablosuz erişim ve savunma sistemleri için mmWave çipleri, anten modülleri ve beamformer ürünleri üretiyor. Photonics bölümünde gelir ağırlıklı olarak lazer donanımı satışından geliyor. Wireless bölümünde ise şirket hem donanım satıyor hem de müşteriye özel ürün geliştirme sözleşmelerinden gelir elde ediyor."}}}, "rest_id": "2075920541320384631", "source": "Twitter Web Client", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "3585", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Honestly, I was completely dumbfounded. I’ve been sitting on a massive book of copper equities for years now, but if you’re long Dr. Copper, you should be aggressively praying that Jensen Huang and Sam Altman don't get locked up and AI data centers don't spontaneously combust. Legacy copper demand has been flatlining for a minute now. We've all heard the "China property sector is toast" macro tape until we're blue in the face. Granted this cycle is heavily supply-driven, but the demand side is anchored to the high tech narrative. It's the energy transition and data centers that are holding up the structural demand thesis. This frontier demand is ramping on a hockey-stick trajectory; EVs were the headline act early on, but grid and transmission capex are rapidly becoming the real heavy lifters. Backstopped by the AI revolution and this insatiable hunger for power, global grid capex is compounding exponentially, and copper is running the textbook picks and shovels play. Also structurally speaking, an EV gobbles up almost 3x the copper intensity of a legacy ICE vehicle, meaning EVs already occupy a massive chunk of the demand pie chart. To top it off AI data centers are more than double the copper-intensity of a vanilla crypto mining warehouse. The data center demand vector is moving in a straight line up and right. And yet, some guys still want Tesla and Nvidia to go to zero? Give me a break. Reserve Protocol has launched five decentralized token funds on BNB Chain, giving investors direct on-chain exposure to the physical infrastructure powering the AI revolution.

The five funds are:

➥ $BUILDOUT — Full AI infrastructure stack
➥ $POWER — Energy and power systems
➥ $PHOTON — Photonics and optical connectivity
➥ $NEOCLOUD — AI compute capacity (neoclouds)
➥ $ROBOTS — Robotics and embodied AI

Each fund is a curated basket of tokenized U.S.-listed companies and trades 24/7 on-chain. Unlike traditional ETFs, these can be minted, redeemed, and traded permissionlessly on BNB Chain.

This launch expands global access to AI infrastructure exposure through decentralized, tokenized products, covering everything from the chips and power to the optics and robots that make advanced AI possible.

text:
NSF has already opened two X-Labs funding opportunities, and each eligible lead organization may submit up to two proposals per topic.

The first topic, Quantum Systems: Interconnects and Integrated Photonics, targets the scale-out bottleneck in quantum computing by supporting modular QPU links, heterogeneous quantum-system interconnects, quantum transducers, low-loss photonics, light sources, switches, and single-photon detectors.

The second topic, Scientific Instrumentation for Sensing and Imaging, focuses on next-generation measurement platforms, including quantum sensors, AI-enabled imaging, molecular-scale detection, deep-tissue imaging, and advanced scientific instruments.

Both programs use the same phased X-Labs structure: up to $1.5 million in Phase 0, followed by $10–50 million per year in Phases 1 and 2.

Assuming the maximum annual award and the full 36-month duration in both later phases, the theoretical maximum reaches $301.5 million per X-Lab.
If a lead organization ultimately secures and fully advances one X-Lab in each of the two topics, the combined theoretical ceiling would be $603 million.
These figures are not guaranteed allocations, because every phase is subject to Go/No-Go review, milestone achievement, negotiation, and the availability of federal funds.
The program is therefore much larger than a conventional NSF research grant and is designed to create independent, full-time organizations capable of building commercially relevant platform technologies.
For comparison, DARPA’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative offers a theoretical maximum of about $316 million per company across all stages.

In scale, X-Labs is therefore approaching QBI, but its focus is broader: QBI validates utility-scale quantum-computer architectures, while X-Labs funds the enabling platforms that could reshape quantum systems, sensing, and scientific instrumentation.

The second topic, Scientific Instrumentation for Sensing and Imaging, focuses on next-generation measurement platforms, including quantum sensors, AI-enabled imaging, molecular-scale detection, deep-tissue imaging, and advanced scientific instruments. Both programs use the same phased X-Labs structure: up to $1.5 million in Phase 0, followed by $10–50 million per year in Phases 1 and 2. Assuming the maximum annual award and the full 36-month duration in both later phases, the theoretical maximum reaches $301.5 million per X-Lab. These figures are not guaranteed allocations, because every phase is subject to Go/No-Go review, milestone achievement, negotiation, and the availability of federal funds. The program is therefore much larger than a conventional NSF research grant and is designed to create independent, full-time organizations capable of building commercially relevant platform technologies. For comparison, DARPA’s Quantum Benchmarking Initiative offers a theoretical maximum of about $316 million per company across all stages. text: A fibre thinner than a human hair can tell you exactly how a structure is bending in real time. Fibre Bragg Gratings shift their reflected wavelength in direct proportion to strain — precise enough to detect sub-millimetre deformation. This is how future infrastructure monitors itself. #FBG #Photonics #StructuralHealthMonitoring #Sensing

text:
AI Infrastructure Spending Continues to Fuel Semiconductor Supply Chain

AI infrastructure stocks moved higher after reports that $META plans to double its in-house computing capacity to 14GW next year, reinforcing expectations that hyperscaler AI capital expenditures remain in a multi-year expansion cycle. At the same time, $MU announced plans to invest more than $250 billion in U.S. semiconductor manufacturing through 2035, further supporting demand across the semiconductor equipment, optical networking, memory, and advanced packaging ecosystem.

For investors, the key takeaway is that AI spending is no longer centered solely on GPUs. Every incremental gigawatt of AI compute requires a massive build-out of servers, networking, optical interconnects, memory, storage, power management, cooling systems, semiconductor manufacturing equipment, and advanced materials.

This broader infrastructure investment benefits companies across the AI supply chain, including $ASML, $COHR, $LITE, $MRVL, $AVGO, $MU, $VRT, $GLW, $POET, $NVDA, $AMD, $ANET, and $SMCI.

Among them, $COHR (Coherent) remains one of the more compelling AI infrastructure beneficiaries. The company supplies critical photonics, optical networking components, lasers, and advanced semiconductor materials that enable high-speed data transmission inside AI data centers. As AI clusters become larger and more bandwidth-intensive, demand for optical connectivity is expected to accelerate.

Fundamentally, $COHR continues to execute well. The company reported 20.5% year-over-year revenue growth in its latest quarter, while Wall Street remains constructive, with TD Cowen, Needham, and Rothschild & Co. maintaining Buy ratings. Current analyst price targets generally range between $395 and $455, implying meaningful upside if AI infrastructure demand remains strong.

Institutional ownership also remains supportive. Recent filings showed $NVDA establishing a significant position in $COHR, while several large asset managers increased their holdings, reflecting continued institutional conviction in AI infrastructure. Although insider selling has continued, the transactions appear consistent with routine profit-taking rather than a deterioration in the company’s business fundamentals.

Investment view: The secular AI infrastructure thesis remains intact. As hyperscalers continue expanding AI data centers, the biggest long-term winners are likely to be companies supplying the critical enabling technologies—not only GPU leaders like $NVDA and $AMD, but also infrastructure providers such as $ASML, $COHR, $LITE, $MRVL, $VRT, $GLW, and $POET that power the broader AI ecosystem.

The company reported 20.5% year-over-year revenue growth in its latest quarter, while Wall Street remains constructive, with TD Cowen, Needham, and Rothschild & Co. maintaining Buy ratings. Current analyst price targets generally range between $395 and $455, implying meaningful upside if AI infrastructure demand remains strong. Institutional ownership also remains supportive. Recent filings showed $NVDA establishing a significant position in $COHR, while several large asset managers increased their holdings, reflecting continued institutional conviction in AI infrastructure. Although insider selling has continued, the transactions appear consistent with routine profit-taking rather than a deterioration in the company’s business fundamentals. Investment view: The secular AI infrastructure thesis remains intact. As hyperscalers continue expanding AI data centers, the biggest long-term winners are likely to be companies supplying the critical enabling technologies—not only GPU leaders like $NVDA and $AMD, but also infrastructure providers such as $ASML, $COHR, $LITE, $MRVL, $VRT, $GLW, and $POET that power the broader AI ecosystem."}}}, "rest_id": "2076732359916060881", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "309", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
BIG BREAKTHROUGH:

Quantum computing just took a meaningful step into real-world biology.

"Danish scientists combined a photonic quantum computer with generative AI to design vaccine peptides, then validated the results in the lab."

"Across 131 HLA types, the hybrid system generated stronger peptide candidates than classical AI for 63% of them, with its greatest advantage appearing when training data were scarce."

"This suggests hybrid Quantum + AI could help accelerate vaccine development, personalized immunotherapy and drug discovery for diseases that currently lack sufficient biological data."

Ai diffusion is unstoppable!

text:
🤖 10 public companies to watch in robotics

Robotics is becoming one of the fastest-growing AI markets. While most attention goes to humanoid robots, the biggest opportunities are spread across sensors, chips, automation, and industrial infrastructure.

🖱 $TER - Tests AI chips and owns Universal Robots, one of the world's leading collaborative robot makers.

🖱 $NOVT - Builds precision photonics and motion-control systems used in surgical and industrial robots.

🖱 $OUST - Develops lidar sensors that help robots and autonomous machines map the physical world.

🖱 $HIMX - Supplies vision and display chips that process images and sensor data for robots.

🖱 $AMBQ - Designs low-power AI chips for robots and edge devices.

🖱 $ON - Expanding its robotics stack through the acquisition of Siyna, adding sensing, connectivity, and edge AI.

🖱 $SYM - Warehouse automation company combining robotics with software for fulfillment centers.

🖱 $VPG - Makes force and pressure sensors that help robots interact with the real world.

🖱 $TSLA - Long-term humanoid robotics play through Optimus, combining AI, hardware, and manufacturing.

🖱 $CCXI - Gives investors exposure to Agility Robotics, the company behind the Digit warehouse robot.

The robotics market extends far beyond humanoids. The biggest winners could be the companies supplying the hardware and infrastructure every robot depends on.

The robotics market extends far beyond humanoids. text:
Morgan Stanley identifies CPO as a key shift in AI data center networks

Driven by power and density constraints
Its main benefit is the reduction of energy consumption in optical interconnections by 50-80%

Key participants:

- Switches: $AVGO and $NVDA

- Optical engines and photonics:

Lightmatter, Ayar Labs, $MRVL/Celestial AI and $POET

- Silicon photonics foundry: $TSMC $GFS and $TSEM

- Laser supply: $LITE $COHR and $SIVE

This is the first time Morgan Stanley mentions $SIVE in its analyses

The inclusion of $SIVE as a key laser provider alongside industry leaders reinforces its positioning in a critical technology for AI infrastructure

Giving it the well deserved importance and exposure and turning it into a key player in photonics infrastructure and AI in general

This opens a clear structural growth potential for $SIVE as CPO is adopted on a large scale

Ayar Labs and $GFS are also named

Two companies that collaborate with $SIVE through which a lot of revenue also enters and that will boost scalability when the CPO ramp begins

This is extremely bullish, no longer a "maybe they achieve something" "maybe they play an important role in the future" scalability

But they KNOW are a crucial and benchmark company for photonics and specifically for CPO

Long $SIVE

text:
Morgan Stanley just laid out the entire CPO supply chain so who's actually in it.

"Co-packaged optics is emerging as a critical architectural shift for AI datacenter networking, driven by the rising power, bandwidth, and density constraints of traditional pluggable optical modules."

CPO can cut optical interconnect power by roughly 50-80% while freeing up front-panel space, which matters as switch bandwidth keeps scaling.

The name list matters more than the thesis at this point : 

> $AVGO and $NVDA in switch platforms. 

> $MRVL and $POET in optical engines and photonics. 

> $TSM GlobalFoundries and Tower in silicon photonics foundry capacity. 

> $LITE , $COHR and $SIVE in laser supply.

This is not  just the obvious mega caps.

text: A fibre thinner than a human hair can tell you exactly how a structure is bending in real time. Fibre Bragg Gratings shift their reflected wavelength in direct proportion to strain — precise enough to detect sub-millimetre deformation. This is how future infrastructure monitors itself. #FBG #Photonics #StructuralHealthMonitoring #Sensing

text:
Agree, and the "stops too early" part is the whole game. The useful question it's how long it stays one and who collects the rent while it lasts.

That mostly sorts the list. Power and interconnect are a durable one, physically capped by grids, permitting and multi-year lead times, so pricing holds. Packaging and photonics are real but catch-up constraints, capacity is coming, so those rents compress.

Memory is the one I genuinely can't resolve, and you're closer to it than I am. Historically it's the definition of cyclical, supply answers and pricing rolls. The structural case, agentic inference making memory demand persistent instead of bursty, is the most interesting counter I've seen, but I keep getting stuck on whether it actually breaks the pattern or just delays the same capex response.

So I'll flip it to you since it's your wheelhouse: what makes this cycle different, if it is? That's the piece that decides whether memory sits in the durable bucket or the mean reverting one.

text:
🚨 QUANTUM STOCKS – SECTOR SELLOFF CONTINUES

Quantum computing stocks are under pressure today as investors take profits across one of the market’s highest-volatility emerging technology themes.

📊 TODAY’S QUANTUM STOCK SNAPSHOT

🔴 $IONQ (IonQ) — $39.35 -8.19%
🔴 $ARQQ (Arqit Quantum) — $17.85 -6.45%
🔴 $QBTS (D-Wave Quantum) — $18.83 -6.27%
🔴 $QUBT (Quantum Computing Inc.) — $8.13 -6.12%
🔴 $RGTI (Rigetti Computing) — $15.54 -6.08%

👉 Core takeaway:
Quantum stocks are broadly lower today, with $IONQ leading the decline (-8.19%). The sector remains highly speculative, with investors balancing long-term quantum potential against near-term profitability challenges.

📈 QUANTUM STACK BREAKDOWN
⚛️ Trapped-Ion Quantum Computing
• $IONQ — IonQ
Leading trapped-ion quantum platform

Focus on enterprise quantum applications
Partnerships across cloud ecosystems

🔬 Quantum Software & Security

• $ARQQ — Arqit Quantum

Quantum encryption and cybersecurity focus

Post-quantum security opportunity

❄️ Quantum Annealing
• $QBTS — D-Wave Quantum
Quantum annealing systems

Optimization-focused applications

🧪 Superconducting Quantum Systems

• $RGTI — Rigetti Computing
Superconducting quantum processors

Full-stack quantum development

💻 Emerging Quantum Platforms

• $QUBT — Quantum Computing Inc.
Quantum software and photonics-based solutions

🏭 Industrial Quantum Exposure

• $HON — Honeywell

Aerospace + industrial technology
Exposure through quantum investments

⚡ WHAT’S DRIVING THE MOVE

🔴 Profit-taking across speculative technology themes

📉 High valuations remain vulnerable in early-stage sectors

⏳ Quantum commercialisation timelines remain uncertain

🤖 Investors favour near-term AI revenue over longer-duration technology bets

Signal:
Quantum remains a potentially transformative technology, but markets are demanding clearer evidence of commercial adoption and revenue growth.
🔥 BULL vs BEAR

🟢 Bull Case
• Quantum breakthroughs accelerate adoption
• Enterprise use cases emerge faster than expected
• Government investment increases
• Quantum security becomes critical in the AI era

🔴 Bear Case
• Commercial timelines remain long
• Many companies remain unprofitable
• Technology competition is intense
• Valuations may run ahead of fundamentals

💭 CONCLUSION
Quantum stocks are seeing a broad pullback today, with all major names in the red.
The long-term opportunity remains enormous, but unlike AI — where demand is already producing billions in revenue — quantum is still in the research, development and early commercialisation phase.
👀 KEY QUESTION:
Will quantum computing become the next AI-like technology revolution, or will investors need to wait years before meaningful returns appear?

text:
At $1.3 billion market cap $SIVE just got a mention from Morgan Stanley alongside giants like $NVDA, $AVGO, $MRVL and $TSM as a key participant in the CPO supply chain.

Their exact words.

“Key participants include Broadcom and NVIDIA in switch platforms, Lightmatter, Ayar Labs, Marvell/Celestial AI, and POET in optical engines and photonics, TSMC, GlobalFoundries, and Tower in silicon photonics foundry capacity, and Lumentum, Coherent, and Sivers in laser supply.”

Sivers. In the same sentence as the biggest names in AI infrastructure.

And here is what makes it even more interesting.

Morgan Stanley listed Ayar Labs in optical engines. $SIVE is Ayar Labs confirmed laser supplier since 2022.

Morgan Stanley listed $GFS in foundry capacity. $SIVE lasers are baked into the $GFS SCALE reference design.

Morgan Stanley listed $POET in optical engines. $SIVE and $POET have a confirmed partnership with production ramping H2 2026.

$SIVE is not just mentioned. They are confirmed at the laser layer of almost every key participant Morgan Stanley named.

At $1.3 billion today.
